| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | RFK Jr just dropped a bomb—and Washington is in panic. This isn't a rumor. Not speculation. These are facts the authorities are desperate to hide. They mocked him for saying the pandemic started in a lab. They sneered when he warned about microplastics in our bodies. They dismissed his claims of FDA corruption. But he was right. Always right. Now his latest warning is darker than anything before. Leaked documents reveal: America’s seniors are quietly being pushed into danger—mysterious illnesses, sudden collapses written off as “normal aging,” and millions funneling into risky prescriptions while the truth remains hidden. If you were born before January 1, 1965 , this is your personal risk. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Mark Kelly
subject: My decision
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. | Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23752%2E4449941%2EiLpVu1) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23752%2E4449941%2EiLpVu1) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71124?t=3&akid=23752%2E4449941%2EiLpVu1) Hey team, it’s Mark Kelly.

Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate.

I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy.

But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something.

So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too.

I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help.

We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.

After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do.

[**I’m asking you to split a donation between Fight For Reform and me, and I hope you know that I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t important.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71124?t=4&akid=23752%2E4449941%2EiLpVu1) | | [DONATE $5 NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71125?amount=5&amounts=25%2C50%2C100%2C250&t=5&akid=23752%2E4449941%2EiLpVu1)

name: The Democratic National Committee
subject: We’re not beholden to an army of dark money billionaires
body:
---
Derek, Next year, we have the chance to win back control of _both_ chambers of Congress by flipping four seats in the Senate and just three seats in the House. And we have a chance to elect Democrats as governors across the country. **We have a very real chance to fight for working families and bring Trump’s presidency to a grinding halt. But _only_ if we keep our momentum from 2025.** But here’s the bad news, Derek: Donald Trump and the GOP’s billionaire donors saw how enthusiastic people were to elect Democrats this year, and they’re getting ready to unleash a **_flood**_ of money in states across the country to stop us. We’re not beholden to an army of dark money billionaires like Donald Trump is. Everything we do — all of our success — is powered by people like **you. [**That’s why we’re asking you to chip in $7 today. To flip 7 seats and gain control of Congress. To stand up for working people all across America. And to build a moat to counter the flood of Republican billionaire cash in the swing states.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?refcode=em_20251218_5568_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5568_22930333_ddM9AK&akid=5568%2E22930333%2EddM9AK) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251218_5568_fr&refcode2=5568_22930333_ddM9AK&akid=5568%2E22930333%2EddM9AK)**

name: Eliza Walton
subject: Peter, will you give now to pass climate legislation in 2026?
body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=ZzlgI4HX+MjKNaYlfGm1qJg/kZ1qP1Wx8xl4CTxoFLXCS//0R6INQjPnCDQNHwswVshIh0Eywzt3aj/NyG5s70VdIKVlf8fEUX8hkNAjwFVAuQ7Bfbtccsyy6U6/qRZO [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=ZzlgI4HX+MjKNaYlfGm1qJg/kZ1qP1Wx8xl4CTxoFLXCS//0R6INQjPnCDQNHwswVshIh0Eywzt3aj/NyG5s70VdIKVlf8fEUX8hkNAjwFVAuQ7Bfbtccsyy6U6/qRZO]
Dear Peter,
As we head into the holidays, I wanted to be sure to catch you, Peter, before then to loop you into all that’s ahead of us — because we need your support. After we turn the page on this year, we jump right into the 2026 legislative session. We don’t have much time to build resources to ensure we have what it takes to pass landmark wildlife and habitat conservation funding, a bill to hold polluters responsible for harming our climate, and to protect investments in Oregon’s climate, natural resources, clean energy, conservation, and wildlife agencies ( see our 2026 priorities [https://olcveducationfund.org/2026-legislative-session/] ). That’s why I’m asking for your generous support now.
Will you make a $50 gift to OLCV now to secure wins for a Healthy Oregon in 2026? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2]
Give now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=ZzlgI4HX+MjKNaYlfGm1qJg/kZ1qP1Wx8xl4CTxoFLXCS//0R6INQjPnCDQNHwswVshIh0Eywzt3aj/NyG5s70VdIKVlf8fEUX8hkNAjwFVAuQ7Bfbtccsyy6U6/qRZO]
We have a very short turnaround time to secure wins for our 2026 Priorities for a Healthy Oregon [https://olcveducationfund.org/2026-legislative-session/] , and it starts next month with January Leg Days. There isn’t much time for us to gather the resources we’ll need, which is why I’m requesting your support now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2] .
But you might be asking — why should I give to OLCV? In response, I’ll let the numbers speak for themselves. During this past session, we not only hit our goals for climate advocacy, but exceeded them.
* Over 14,000 of our members emailed their legislator, surpassing our goal of 10,000;
* We recruited 70 volunteer leaders;
* And our volunteers completed over 500 shifts, speaking to lawmakers about our priorities.
Yes, our legislators failed to meet our calls for climate action, but the one thing that didn’t fail is OLCV and our members. We’re ready to return this year with more power for our climate than ever, because our state work grows increasingly important with each federal cut made to our climate. But, we can’t do it without you.
Give now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=ZzlgI4HX+MjKNaYlfGm1qJg/kZ1qP1Wx8xl4CTxoFLXCS//0R6INQjPnCDQNHwswVshIh0Eywzt3aj/NyG5s70VdIKVlf8fEUX8hkNAjwFVAuQ7Bfbtccsyy6U6/qRZO]
A gift of $50 to OLCV today will lead us into lasting and monumental change for our climate in 2026. Challenges are surely ahead of us, but so is great opportunity. One thing’s for certain: climate action is possible, but only when generous donors like you, Peter, step forward. Make your generous contribution today to build a sustainable and livable future for all! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2]
In solidarity,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Eliza.png]
Eliza Walton (she/they)
OLCV Coalition Director
P.S.: We are in the big push for end of year fundraising. If you would no longer like to receive emails related to end of year fundraising, please fill out this form here to opt-out [https://secure.everyaction.com/c2_U_ki-NEyU8Gdkttquug2] but still remain on our general email list!

clean_body: [] Dear Peter, As we head into the holidays, I wanted to be sure to catch you, Peter, before then to loop you into all that’s ahead of us — because we need your support. After we turn the page on this year, we jump right into the 2026 legislative session. We don’t have much time to build resources to ensure we have what it takes to pass landmark wildlife and habitat conservation funding, a bill to hold polluters responsible for harming our climate, and to protect investments in Oregon’s climate, natural resources, clean energy, conservation, and wildlife agencies ( see our 2026 priorities [] ). That’s why I’m asking for your generous support now. Will you make a $50 gift to OLCV now to secure wins for a Healthy Oregon in 2026? [] Give now [] We have a very short turnaround time to secure wins for our 2026 Priorities for a Healthy Oregon [] , and it starts next month with January Leg Days. There isn’t much time for us to gather the resources we’ll need, which is why I’m requesting your support now [] . But you might be asking — why should I give to OLCV? In response, I’ll let the numbers speak for themselves. During this past session, we not only hit our goals for climate advocacy, but exceeded them. Over 14,000 of our members emailed their legislator, surpassing our goal of 10,000; We recruited 70 volunteer leaders; * And our volunteers completed over 500 shifts, speaking to lawmakers about our priorities. Yes, our legislators failed to meet our calls for climate action, but the one thing that didn’t fail is OLCV and our members. We’re ready to return this year with more power for our climate than ever, because our state work grows increasingly important with each federal cut made to our climate. But, we can’t do it without you. Give now [] A gift of $50 to OLCV today will lead us into lasting and monumental change for our climate in 2026. Challenges are surely ahead of us, but so is great opportunity. One thing’s for certain: climate action is possible, but only when generous donors like you, Peter, step forward. Make your generous contribution today to build a sustainable and livable future for all! [] In solidarity, [] Eliza Walton (she/they) OLCV Coalition Director P.S.: We are in the big push for end of year fundraising. If you would no longer like to receive emails related to end of year fundraising, please fill out this form here to opt-out [] but still remain on our general email list! [] [] [] [] Oregon League of Conservation Voters 1110 SE Alder Suite 220 Portland, OR 97214 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Haley Stevens
subject: Why I introduced articles of impeachment against RFK Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
There might not be anyone more dangerous in Trump’s Cabinet

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1675%2E49239%2ETSe45P)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10593?t=2&akid=1675%2E49239%2ETSe45P)
---
Hi there, null,

Did you catch _The New York Times_ story about why I introduced articles of impeachment against RFK Jr.?
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10594?t=3&akid=1675%2E49239%2ETSe45P)
The truth is, there might not be anyone more dangerous in Trump’s Cabinet right now.
RFK Jr. has fired the CDC director, leading to department-wide confusion and uncertainty. He’s purged essential government scientists and doctors. He’s gutted research into cancer, HIV, addiction, and mRNA technology – research that saves lives and powers Michigan’s innovation economy. **_He misled members of the U.S. Senate under oath._**
I called for his resignation months ago. But when I saw him standing next to Trump as he told pregnant women not to take Tylenol – claiming, with zero proof, that it could cause autism – I hit my limit.

I’m serious about defending public health and Michigan families – that’s why we need to hold RFK Jr. accountable. And that means removing him from office. We need thoughtful leaders who put science over BS, facts over conspiracy theories, and people over politics. RFK Jr. has proven – _over and over again_ – that he is not one of them.
That’s why I intend to lead the charge to remove him from office – but it's not enough.

We must elect leaders who believe that science should drive policy, who will fight for our families, our health, and our future. That's a big part of why I'm running for Senate.
**As one of the only open seats ranked as a “Toss Up,” Michigan is a critical battleground in our fight to flip the Senate blue and provide a much-needed check on Trump’s anti-science agenda. Republicans spent nearly $80 million to win here last year, and they almost succeeded – so I’m personally asking:[Will you chip in $5 or more now to help me raise the resources it’ll take to win and help Democrats reclaim the Senate majority?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10594?t=4&akid=1675%2E49239%2ETSe45P)**
_Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue._ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10595?t=5&akid=1675%2E49239%2ETSe45P)

---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10597?t=7&akid=1675%2E49239%2ETSe45P)

---
Thank you,

Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10598?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=9&akid=1675%2E49239%2ETSe45P) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10593?t=10&akid=1675%2E49239%2ETSe45P)

Thank you,
Haley

This email is unlike anything I've ever sent you before.

No, this isn't about a campaign or an election.

I'm sending you an urgent message about an organization I'm personally supporting, The Atlas Society.

clean_body: I’m sending you an urgent message about an organization I’m personally supporting, The Atlas Society. I’m personally supporting The Atlas Society—please read and consider donating. | This email is unlike anything I've ever sent you before. No, this isn't about a campaign or an election. I'm sending you an urgent message about an organization I'm personally supporting, The Atlas Society. Their CEO, Jennifer Grossman, has been my friend for thirty years -- we got our start in cable news together in the mid-1990s, and she later hired my firm to do polling when she worked at Dole Food Company. Through ups and downs, we've remained friends. Today, she's the CEO of The Atlas Society, a non-profit organization whose mission is to spread Ayn Rand's message of capitalism, personal responsibility, reason, and entrepreneurship to young people. Why is The Atlas Society's mission so important? If my career in polling and serving as President Trump's 2016 Campaign Manager taught me anything, it's this: Americans are desperate to hear the unfiltered truth. This is why we need more young people to hear The Atlas Society 's message. Here is what Jennifer and her organization are fighting for: Free market capitalism Respect for achievement and success Free speech The future of young people and our great country Our culture is pushing a twisted message of victimization, envy, woke nonsense, socialism, class warfare, and hatred for our country and its founding principles. Young people need an alternative. The Atlas Society spreads Ayn Rand's message to millions of young people via social media, video content, graphic novels, conferences, and so much more. I promised Jennifer I would help her raise $25,000 for The Atlas Society, which is why I am reaching out to you. Please consider making a donation by using one of the links I've copied below: You may be surprised, but when young people hear Ayn Rand's message of entrepreneurship, refusing to be a victim, and personal achievement, they are inspired and want to learn more. Yes, politics are important. But politics are downstream from culture. And culture is downstream from philosophy. The Atlas Society is on the frontlines of fighting in the philosophical culture war, which may determine the fate of the country's future. We CANNOT give up the culture or give up young people. This is more important than a campaign or a single election. You and I must invest in reaching young people with this message. Please don't wait to take action. My goal is to raise this $25,000 ASAP within a 48-hour period. Can I count on you to make a donation? Kellyanne Conway, Former Senior Counselor to President Trump The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible. 1038 Miwok Dr Lodi, CA 95240

body:
You can bet this was ordered by Donald Trump and carried out by Pete Hegseth. But right now, that's besides the point.
The Department of Defense is coming after me because they don’t like what I have to say.
[](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251218_1.iKPlIh&refcode=em20251218-1&t=1&akid=4083%2E582294%2ESJaOpj)
When I was 22 years old, I swore an oath to the Constitution. I've upheld that oath every day since.
If Trump is trying to intimidate me from doing my job, it won’t work.
I’ve given too much to this country to be silenced by bullies who care more about their own power than about protecting the Constitution.
Call this what it is: **Political persecution.**
You can bet this was ordered by Donald Trump and carried out by Pete Hegseth. But right now, that's besides the point.
**[The point is that I need you to chip in whatever you can afford to Chris Pappas and me so we have the resources necessary to fight back against what comes next.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251218_1.iKPlIh&refcode=em20251218-1&t=2&akid=4083%2E582294%2ESJaOpj)**

name: Rob Sand
subject: this email is a little longer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rob Sand for Iowa
body:
[ https://act.robsand.com/go/918?t=1001&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ][IMG]

Folks, I want to give you fair warning that this email is longer
than usual, but I do hope you’re able to spare a couple of minutes to read
the whole thing.

With less than a year until Election Day, my team and I are working on our
game plan for the remainder of this neck-and-neck race, and the outline of
that plan is below.

If you’re in a hurry and you don’t have time to read it all, my feelings
won’t be hurt (too badly). [ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1014&amount=25&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Having said that, if you’re able to chip in
a quick grassroots donation to power our campaign before closing this
email, that would really make my day.

[ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1015&amount=25&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]DONATE

If you’ve made it this far — hey 👋 thanks for being here.

Right, so this is obviously oversimplifying things a bit, but in the
spirit of not emailing you an entire novel, I did my best to distill our
campaign’s plan into three action items:

Everything, Everywhere, All at Once

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

The most important thing we can do to set ourselves up for success is talk
to as many people as possible, in as many places as possible.

We’re talking to Republicans, Democrats, Independents, and anyone in
between.

Look, people get their news and consume media differently. While I’ve
already spoken to loads of people in person, some folks probably first
heard about my campaign on social media, while others might have learned
about it through TV, their newspaper, or the radio.

The point here is that there are still a TON of people who’ve yet to hear
from us and others still who might not even know much about the election
or my background.

We know that once folks hear about my vision for a better, more equitable
Iowa that puts people over politics, they’re very, very likely to support
our campaign.

That’s why even though we wrapped my 100 Town Hall Tour, I’m already
looking forward to doing it again in 2026. It’s also why we’re training
volunteers, recruiting and training staff, and running a strong digital
program.

Focus on the Issues that Matter

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Everywhere I go, Iowans — regardless of their political leanings — tell me
they’re sick of partisan talking points and a government that doesn’t work
for them.

They understand that partisan insiders and special interests are pulling
the strings, gaming the system, and putting politics over people — all
while pitting communities against one another.

That’s why while they’re in Washington, hiding from their constituents,
I’m on the ground having tough but necessary conversations about the
issues that actually matter.

Folks are worried about massive cuts to food assistance programs and
losing their health care coverage.

They’re concerned that we’re losing jobs and opportunities to neighboring
states as our economy continues to struggle.

They don’t like the government underfunding our public school system in
favor of a voucher program with zero transparency.

That’s why we’re talking about bringing jobs back to Iowa and making sure
our kids have the same opportunities we did.

We’ve got a plan to bring opportunity and innovation back to our state and
make it a place where folks want to stay and raise a family.

Build the Biggest, Most Vibrant Grassroots Coalition in Iowa History

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Traveling the state, meeting with voters, having important conversations,
holding town halls and other community events... It's a lot.

But it’s 100% necessary and how we’re going to win.

Taking on the powers that be and the partisan insiders won’t be easy, and
they’re backed by wealthy special interests, but we’ve got something they
don’t:

This grassroots team.

It’s growing bigger by the day as more folks hear from us, but we can’t
afford to slow down now. We need to continue growing our grassroots
network, laying the groundwork for victory, and empowering people to have
conversations about the importance of this race.

We’ve got a plan to win this thing, and I’m confident that if we can
execute on all three of these action items, we’ll be in good shape.

[ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1016&amount=25&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Can I count on your support to help put our plan into action and put
ourselves on solid footing as this race continues to heat up? If so,
please chip in $25 or $50 today to help out!

If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately.

[ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1017&express_lane=true&amount=25&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Chip in $25 [ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1018&express_lane=true&amount=50&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Chip in $50
[ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1019&express_lane=true&amount=100&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Chip in $100 [ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1020&express_lane=true&amount=200&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Chip in $200
[ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1021&express_lane=true&amount=250&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Chip in $250 [ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1012&amount=35&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Other Amount

I really appreciate you taking the time to read all this!

—Rob Sand

[ https://act.robsand.com/go/4560?t=1013&amount=25&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ][IMG]

[ https://act.robsand.com/go/918?t=1002&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ][IMG]

Rob has built a coalition of support that crosses party lines because he
knows we can only move Iowa forward together. If you’re able to support
his campaign today you can chip in here:
[ https://act.robsand.com/go/918?t=1003&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Chip in a one-time donation
[ https://act.robsand.com/go/919?t=1004&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]Chip in a monthly recurring donation!

We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in
touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails,
[ https://act.robsand.com/signup/fewer_emails/?t=1005&akid=782%2E412644%2EbchK6z ]click here.

clean_body: [ ][IMG] Folks, I want to give you fair warning that this email is longer than usual, but I do hope you’re able to spare a couple of minutes to read the whole thing. With less than a year until Election Day, my team and I are working on our game plan for the remainder of this neck-and-neck race, and the outline of that plan is below. If you’re in a hurry and you don’t have time to read it all, my feelings won’t be hurt (too badly). [ ]Having said that, if you’re able to chip in a quick grassroots donation to power our campaign before closing this email, that would really make my day. [ ]DONATE If you’ve made it this far — hey 👋 thanks for being here. Right, so this is obviously oversimplifying things a bit, but in the spirit of not emailing you an entire novel, I did my best to distill our campaign’s plan into three action items: Everything, Everywhere, All at Once The most important thing we can do to set ourselves up for success is talk to as many people as possible, in as many places as possible. We’re talking to Republicans, Democrats, Independents, and anyone in between. Look, people get their news and consume media differently. While I’ve already spoken to loads of people in person, some folks probably first heard about my campaign on social media, while others might have learned about it through TV, their newspaper, or the radio. The point here is that there are still a TON of people who’ve yet to hear from us and others still who might not even know much about the election or my background. We know that once folks hear about my vision for a better, more equitable Iowa that puts people over politics, they’re very, very likely to support our campaign. That’s why even though we wrapped my 100 Town Hall Tour, I’m already looking forward to doing it again in 2026. It’s also why we’re training volunteers, recruiting and training staff, and running a strong digital program. Focus on the Issues that Matter Everywhere I go, Iowans — regardless of their political leanings — tell me they’re sick of partisan talking points and a government that doesn’t work for them. They understand that partisan insiders and special interests are pulling the strings, gaming the system, and putting politics over people — all while pitting communities against one another. That’s why while they’re in Washington, hiding from their constituents, I’m on the ground having tough but necessary conversations about the issues that actually matter. Folks are worried about massive cuts to food assistance programs and losing their health care coverage. They’re concerned that we’re losing jobs and opportunities to neighboring states as our economy continues to struggle. They don’t like the government underfunding our public school system in favor of a voucher program with zero transparency. That’s why we’re talking about bringing jobs back to Iowa and making sure our kids have the same opportunities we did. We’ve got a plan to bring opportunity and innovation back to our state and make it a place where folks want to stay and raise a family. Build the Biggest, Most Vibrant Grassroots Coalition in Iowa History Traveling the state, meeting with voters, having important conversations, holding town halls and other community events... It's a lot. But it’s 100% necessary and how we’re going to win. Taking on the powers that be and the partisan insiders won’t be easy, and they’re backed by wealthy special interests, but we’ve got something they don’t: This grassroots team. It’s growing bigger by the day as more folks hear from us, but we can’t afford to slow down now. We need to continue growing our grassroots network, laying the groundwork for victory, and empowering people to have conversations about the importance of this race. We’ve got a plan to win this thing, and I’m confident that if we can execute on all three of these action items, we’ll be in good shape. [ ]Can I count on your support to help put our plan into action and put ourselves on solid footing as this race continues to heat up? If so, please chip in $25 or $50 today to help out! If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. [ ]Chip in $25 [ ]Chip in $50 [ ]Chip in $100 [ ]Chip in $200 [ ]Chip in $250 [ ]Other Amount I really appreciate you taking the time to read all this! —Rob Sand [ ][IMG] [ ][IMG] Rob has built a coalition of support that crosses party lines because he knows we can only move Iowa forward together. If you’re able to support his campaign today you can chip in here: [ ]Chip in a one-time donation [ ]Chip in a monthly recurring donation! We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [ ]click here. You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time: [ ]Bluesky [ ]Facebook [ ]Instagram [ ]X (Twitter) [ ]Website Paid for by Rob Sand for Iowa Rob Sand for Iowa P.O. Box 8382 Des Moines, IA, 50301

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: A note for you at a pivotal moment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elise for Governor
body:
We’re in danger of falling behind...
| **__A Personal Note from Elise Stefanik_ _**
---
Fellow American,
---
Our chances of beating Kathy Hochul depend on hitting _ALL_ of our fundraising benchmarks between now and Election Day.
---
But as it currently stands, we’re in danger of falling behind just days before our most important benchmark yet...
---
**_...our _FIRST_ and _ONLY_ end-of-year deadline!_**
---
Unless we raise another **_$19,055_** before tonight’s mid-month cutoff, we’ll officially enter the final stretch of 2025 behind budget!
---
With the holidays coming up, this would be the worst possible time to fall behind.
---
**To escape this looming crisis, I’m turning to my TOP grassroots supporters like _YOU_ for last-minute help reaching this vital goal. **
---
_As always: If you’ve given all that you can, or can’t afford to contribute right now, please simply disregard what I’m about to ask..._
---
But at this late hour, you’re truly my last hope, Fellow American.
---
[**So please, if you can, make a contribution before the mid-month deadline at 11:59 P.M. name: PoliticsPA
subject: What Makes a Good Budget in Pennsylvania?
body:
A candid, bipartisan look at Pennsylvania’s budget battles | | | |
---
| | | **Big Names Make News**
---
| “The governor continues to lead this Commonwealth. He continues to be a leader in this country. His work on the budget is just another testament to his leadership ability to bring sides together.” -Rep. Jordan Harris [[Watch](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=adb305690a&e=b7b3eddce2)]

“I’ve done budgets for a long time. You only can use one-time sources of revenue, and anything that’s taken out of that, as a one-time infusion of cash, and so it would grow your structural deficit even further.” -Senator Scott Martin [[Watch](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4a6cf65cd5&e=b7b3eddce2)]
---
|
* * *
---
| **What makes a good budget in PA? | Voices of Reason feat. Rep. Jordan Harris and Sen. Scott Martin**
---
| [ ](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=356cb2c50f&e=b7b3eddce2 "What makes a good budget in PA? | Voices of Reason feat. Rep. Jordan Harris and Sen. Scott Martin")
---
|
* * *
---
| **Maps You Need to See**
---
| Now that the Big Beautiful Bill has been put into action, what might these changes do to your taxes as a resident of Pennsylvania. Politics PA calculated that all individual tax filers throughout the country, the average tax cut per taxpayer will be $3,752 in 2026. But what does that mean for you? Take a look at Pennsylvania’s county-by-county tax cut for 2026.
---
| [ ](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a68f30255e&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
| [CLICK HERE FOR THE INTERACTIVE MAP](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=d5f13ae722&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
* * *
---
| **In Case You Missed It...**
---
| The _Voices of Reason_ podcast has covered the most important issues in Pennsylvania politics – from [education funding](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a521a4daa2&e=b7b3eddce2) to [trust in elections](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e67290b442&e=b7b3eddce2). Secretary of the Commonwealth [Al Schmidt](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=98cc2f6a26&e=b7b3eddce2), PA Treasurer [Stacy Garrity](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=656528b9ec&e=b7b3eddce2), and House Appropriations Chairman [Jordan Harris](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=8cf5a43ad6&e=b7b3eddce2) are just a few of many influential guests who have joined us for exclusive interviews and roundtable discussions.
Visit the _Voice of Reason_ [YouTube page](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=36e55c7532&e=b7b3eddce2) to experience all the great moments you missed.
---
|
* * *
---
| **Get in Touch**
---
| We like smart ideas and want to hear from you.

Send feedback and inquire about appearing on _Voices of Reason_ , at [press@politicspa.com](mailto:press@politicspa.com?subject=Voices%20of%20Reason%20Podcast%20Inquiry).
---
|
---
| | | | | | [](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=71997001d4&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
[](https://onpattison.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=7754122354&e=b7b3eddce2)
---

name: Alexandra Rojas
subject: BREAKING: Justice Democrats endorses Junaid Ahmed in IL-08
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

BREAKING, Peter:

Today, Justice Democrats is proud to announce our eighth and final
endorsement before the new year: Junaid Ahmed in Illinois’s 8th
congressional district!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251218-ja.hkH07M&refcode=em20251218-37288&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37288%2E1203867%2EcoJLW6 ]Justice Democrats 2026 Endorsed Junaid Ahmed for U.S. Congress in IL-08

Junaid is a progressive community leader and small-business owner running
to stand up to the Trump administration and turn the page on corporate
special interests who put profit over people.

This is an open House seat, with current Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi running
for Senate, so Junaid’s running in a crowded primary full of corporate
candidates — including one former Congresswoman who has taken hundreds of
thousands of dollars from AIPAC’s friends in the Israel lobby.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251218-ja.hkH07M&refcode=em20251218-37288&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=37288%2E1203867%2EcoJLW6 ]Will you join us in standing with Junaid by making a grassroots
contribution now? 100% of your donation will go toward his people-powered
campaign.

Junaid is the son of immigrant working-class parents who moved to the
Chicagoland area when he was a child. Growing up, he watched his dad work
multiple jobs to make ends meet and provide a better life for his family,
so he knows the struggle of Chicago’s working families.

After Junaid started at community college and went on to study computer
science at DePaul University, he began his career as a software developer
before building his own small business tech company, which currently
employs over 20 people in the state.

When the COVID-19 epidemic upended lives in March 2020, Junaid helped
launch a nonprofit called Chi-Care to deliver hot meals and survival
supplies directly to unhoused neighbors. What started as one truck in
Belmont now runs a daily fleet stretching from Chicago’s North Side to its
southern suburbs and has delivered over 1 million meals.

Beyond Chi-Care, Junaid has supported organizations establishing
transitional housing for women and families fleeing domestic violence and
worked for decades to organize his community around issues like healthcare
access, climate change, and immigration reform. He’s also stood up to ICE
and successfully protested an ICE detention center in 2021, a fight that
has only worsened with Trump’s second administration.

Junaid stands up and fights for what he believes in, and he’s ready to
bring that moral courage and commitment to the working class to Congress.
If we rally around Junaid in this race, he’ll join the rest of our
progressive, working-class bloc in fighting for an economy that works for
us all, healthcare as a human right, quality public education, and a just
immigration system.

On the other hand, one of his most well-funded opponents – Melissa Bean —
is a former Congresswoman who was closely aligned with AIPAC, took
hundreds of thousands of dollars from the Israel lobby, voted against 9/11
first responders' healthcare and marriage equality, and was known as “Wall
Street’s Favorite Democrat.”

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251218-ja.hkH07M&refcode=em20251218-37288&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1004&akid=37288%2E1203867%2EcoJLW6 ]We don’t need another corporate, do-nothing Democrat backed by AIPAC in
Congress: we need Junaid. Let’s kick off our eighth primary endorsement on
a strong note and build the resources he needs to win: pitch in
$5 or more to Junaid’s campaign!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251218-ja.hkH07M&refcode=em20251218-37288&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1023&akid=37288%2E1203867%2EcoJLW6 ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Alexandra Rojas
Executive Director

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251218-37288-footer&t=1024&refcode2=37288_1203867_coJLW6&akid=37288%2E1203867%2EcoJLW6

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=37288%2E1203867%2EcoJLW6

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster, PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

As an Inner Circle Advisory Board member, you’ll receive: ✅ Breaking News Trump Alerts before the fake news media twists the truth ✅ Private Strategy Updates straight from HQ ✅ First Access to brand new merch before the general public ✅ As soon as you activate your membership I'll ship a Red MAGA Hat Ornament to your doorstep! But listen closely: you must claim your before midnight. If you don’t, your spot may be given to another patriot! This is the highest honor I can give my supporters. Don’t let it slip away. – Donald J. Trump | | | | | Contributions to Trump National Committee JFC Inc. are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. All contributions are subject to the limits and prohibitions of the Federal Election Campaign Act. Contributions from corporations, foreign nationals (i.e., without “green cards”), federal government contractors, and other federally impermissible sources are strictly prohibited. Contributions made in the name of, or refunded by, any other person are unlawful. | Paid for by Trump National Committee JFC, Inc., P.O. Box 509, Arlington, VA 22216. Not Authorized by any Candidate or Candidate’s Committee.

name: Movement 4 Freedom
subject: Our December Monthly Meeting is tonight!
body:
You're invited!

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/2534319326cf/tockcw1cwy-224?e=81ab29c4d2)
---
[|
---](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=56f04660ab&e=81ab29c4d2)
| | | | | | Peter,
---
| | We’re closing out the year with a powerful lineup, and you’re invited to join us!
On **Thursday, December 18th at 8PM EST,** the Movement for Freedom is hosting our Monthly Members & Supporters Meeting, where we bring our community together to connect, organize, and stay grounded in the work ahead.This month’s call is one you won’t want to miss.
---
| | | | | | | | | | | [RSVP TODAY](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=2f2aa7ca49&e=81ab29c4d2)
---
| | **📣 Candidate Spotlight: Hartzell Gray** We’re proud to share that Hartzell Gray, our newly endorsed candidate for Missouri’s 4th District, will join us. Hartzell is a lifelong organizer who fights for his community with the same conviction he brings to his campaign — for living wages, Medicare for All, housing as a human right, LGBTQIA+ rights, and a future where working people come first. Come hear directly from him about building people-powered change and what’s next for his campaign.
**🤝 Partner Highlight: Youth Demand DC** We’re also partnering with Youth Demand DC, a dynamic youth-led organization pushing for justice, accountability, and an end to the war on Palestinians. They’ll share updates on their ongoing campaigns, and how our community can plug in.This movement is strongest when we work together. So join us! Your voice and presence matter.
👉 [RSVP now for the December 18th call at 8PM ET.](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=fab0f96111&e=81ab29c4d2)
Let’s close out the year rooted in community, clarity, and action.
---
| |
_**The Movement for Freedom Team**_
---
|
---
|
---
| | | | | | | [|
---](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=d0f5379178&e=81ab29c4d2)
| | _Copyright (C) 2025 Movement 4 Freedom. All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

Our mailing address is:
Movement 4 Freedom PO Box 33079 Washington, DC 20033 USA

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/profile?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=6276f39f53&e=81ab29c4d2&c=231f808ded) or [unsubscribe](https://movement4freedom.us16.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c20cdecc59106d39c65725a01&id=6276f39f53&t=b&e=81ab29c4d2&c=231f808ded)
---
|

name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: This is what gave Charlie strength
body:
One day a week. A radical act of trust.
Charlie’s final message—support Turning Point USA & receive his last book.
| | [ ](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=04f854de368f430c9a99c0617bf69014&_e=fhLOVy3ewf5_jY4sS_ntRcRVqqBcorSWQr8viloTaQtgreadnLFBKBwyB4h5FF74PbCbWQdox5VZ47btiBWj_borpZgzqkpSelljy2i_nXKaJxxiJZH_btKUym-x2PqFpxpOXAt9Hrp5tQSWPlZXDCQk8NzxV5aOMUQHT_ivD4AFiJ6SujjKW4rJD-R_wRdVyoRNEHkKwBdM89F-Md_xsXQ93v8J9IIxwSmVnncIiq1YWmwKg52QLCBMCZGh0XCn1hKAR8iVA_LcPckt3TxU-gd2e0-HnXUUur3NhyeAQzDsEByT1WutPc1dcAXcxHxg-ytrE6YABLUxli9fdWxaLFn7MmK0y72Uek8i86B89xJ9BKYPaEpEhzjGDGJoXWEpIACiE4x-AQXg49az_u9BgQqXXT-AdF9DSA1-N5vnsdSVKm3QKRIlLFx06-JcrnXPiDRrZX1aclB7Md2NaGGQM8s6dV4kKkjD2QX8SyygeW4GZ9YvpY0pWut0bk_Qq-aEOEXISI4_WAyCGcHe2uim6zJSaDyKmb6Vw60vTITvhZ84fxZABpotBnSJbFN8uW0CR6hedBGj5IDRY446mXtHivYJ4LG6znBV5DZgz2hMWQIcYXTHGloONCDAm5_eYWoqXDgjAbrUFv8USqG7OvLlDmjfBaFyTCwFh7piWm1smJaHJ2WakpJphSPgM15X5-Ax)
---
| Fellow Patriot,
---
| Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud.

He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus.

He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness.

And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible.

I saw it up close -- _Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. _

But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything.

_**He started observing the Sabbath. **_

Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance.

_But as an act of obedience. _

As a way of saying, **_" God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." _**

Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped.

He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield.

No email. No media. No work.

He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children.

It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much.

But over time, it changed him.

And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned...
---

| This was Charlie's final message. His final act of trust.

A book not about politics... but about peace.

**It 's called: _Stop, in the Name of God._**

**[And right now, when you make a gift of ANY amount to Turning Point USA, we’ll send you a copy — as our thank-you for carrying Charlie’s mission forward.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=04f854de368f430c9a99c0617bf69014&_e=fhLOVy3ewf5_jY4sS_ntRcRVqqBcorSWQr8viloTaQtgreadnLFBKBwyB4h5FF74PbCbWQdox5VZ47btiBWj_borpZgzqkpSelljy2i_nXKaJxxiJZH_btKUym-x2PqFpxpOXAt9Hrp5tQSWPlZXDCQk8NzxV5aOMUQHT_ivD4AFiJ6SujjKW4rJD-R_wRdVyoRNEHkKwBdM89F-Md_xsXQ93v8J9IIxwSmVnncIiq1c1W8Q-2_FhO6DXJc6vFiPvsFZuLfLQTzupqVRScjQ9ku-7xO4TL0E2opU4CT6d4L6kipWTELXOIsVVl-GhGCScqGybL2GtQEzpGNP3dWQyS9qbobxyqVIDsOK4lN2Q8fDlNJG-XVolJRwi3TxxzvdR4kqc2pNe-EBczQBoPEnU2w5DffLgVK7iHySSywb7iR38zbwCnd8OMDL4ARA7zEAm2YB1BKtzy5Am7ymfz-pg8l_0jKf9bK3pEXTcRnLrTRrNQwRKaw2e9QtubGtoiDL3ZwWr9xjhjTU0ViNhthPV00Sbwa1jROliArke3JaM62H_zp8PxA9DZ0no8Hz7hkkGZ6CsKh1cqE_jic1obptFQZyoNlSmYb3I6odO2NDapo6ayS0P_nMdzF6eCzc5GsgF1RR5wSNxkaTfjkXXtVKLkomzeUcUd8jCQrYsgXumcDllxiJXyUawzrttvjK8OJi)**

| Fellow Patriot, like I mentioned, this isn't a political book.

It's not a culture war manifesto.

It's a call to stop.

To rest. To breathe. To return to God.

**Because Charlie believed America hadn 't just forgotten truth... It had forgotten trust. **

Trust that the world keeps spinning when you step away.

Trust that God is in control.

Trust that rest is not weakness -- it's worship.

**In _Stop, in the Name of God,_ you'll discover: **

🔥 How Charlie reclaimed the Sabbath in a world built on burnout

🔥 What sacred rest gave back to him -- as a father, husband, and believer

🔥 Why he believed this message could spark revival in a restless generation

Charlie wrote this book because he knew what it meant to nearly lose his peace -- and what it felt like to find it again.

That's the heartbeat of his final message.

**[To claim your copy of this life-changing book, simply make a generous gift in any amount to Turning Point USA today.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=04f854de368f430c9a99c0617bf69014&_e=fhLOVy3ewf5_jY4sS_ntRcRVqqBcorSWQr8viloTaQtgreadnLFBKBwyB4h5FF74PbCbWQdox5VZ47btiBWj_borpZgzqkpSelljy2i_nXKaJxxiJZH_btKUym-x2PqFpxpOXAt9Hrp5tQSWPlZXDCQk8NzxV5aOMUQHT_ivD4AFiJ6SujjKW4rJD-R_wRdVyoRNEHkKwBdM89F-Md_xsXQ93v8J9IIxwSmVnncIiq1iK-n0DBamxc18BLiPXn9mFyFc-i057meI19JjbwwmKfRiBv6kqlNNvvWTxrb19yeLDvy6iLuoBPA3zYKLNL_03oyrvuOxr_Ha5fgXUusz7JPKwxEpcz_SvnnBUJtdj4ExrozUbuDpVIywJDejSsAKWz_RHBFhCaNvkNQVUVrbX8fG3TC7NoxVkx9PY9diD9wm2KFNeGCQPacTqx9xhoSmaNLbBjBMTfsPg1rM-NfNV0S6m4T0XKMlNjQ61F99AI2cKw9Dy4uUo8vqeLpkYo8p3H6Y1KY3Ezpw7ucx2RFPVYifO214rnwZPj42atv94HPPzM6_LkbKoZnzYSsSyOYYs2yKES6nsXvX3-zp26nYEbOlxU7MNVO2l48iz0MPxmiK3XcZ18uFaaQgiZeLILoVtA2YpX5SVmb4sSnyClrgnEpTsZOXilSbd6BFeh8bnKHHkyDh5EIU002PqaEmDx1-)**

| Charlie Kirk didn't waste his time on earth.

He left behind a family. He left behind a movement. And he left behind this truth...

For anyone ready to step off the treadmill... and walk back toward what's eternal.

Fellow Patriot, thank you -- in advance -- for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage.

These are the very things Charlie gave his life for.

May the message he left behind lead you -- just as it led him -- to a deeper trust in God...

A stronger love for your family...

And a renewed peace in your own soul.
---
| **For Charlie, **

**Justin Streiff **
Chief Operating Officer
Turning Point USA

clean_body: One day a week. A radical act of trust. Charlie’s final message—support Turning Point USA & receive his last book. | | | Fellow Patriot, | Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud. He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus. He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness. And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible. I saw it up close -- Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything. He started observing the Sabbath. Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance. But as an act of obedience. As a way of saying, " God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped. He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield. No email. No media. No work. He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children. It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much. But over time, it changed him. And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned... | | This was Charlie's final message. His final act of trust. A book not about politics... but about peace. It 's called: Stop, in the Name of God. | | Fellow Patriot, like I mentioned, this isn't a political book. It's not a culture war manifesto. It's a call to stop. To rest. To breathe. To return to God. Because Charlie believed America hadn 't just forgotten truth... It had forgotten trust. Trust that the world keeps spinning when you step away. Trust that God is in control. Trust that rest is not weakness -- it's worship. In Stop, in the Name of God, you'll discover: 🔥 How Charlie reclaimed the Sabbath in a world built on burnout 🔥 What sacred rest gave back to him -- as a father, husband, and believer 🔥 Why he believed this message could spark revival in a restless generation Charlie wrote this book because he knew what it meant to nearly lose his peace -- and what it felt like to find it again. That's the heartbeat of his final message. - | | Charlie Kirk didn't waste his time on earth. He left behind a family. He left behind a movement. And he left behind this truth... For anyone ready to step off the treadmill... and walk back toward what's eternal. Fellow Patriot, thank you -- in advance -- for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage. These are the very things Charlie gave his life for. May the message he left behind lead you -- just as it led him -- to a deeper trust in God... A stronger love for your family... And a renewed peace in your own soul. | For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating Officer Turning Point USA | | | P.S.Stop, in the Name of God is Charlie Kirk's final message -- written in obedience, shaped by Scripture, and grounded in trust in the Lord. | | 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

Peter, Fair representation for Black and Hispanic voters is under attack in Florida: | **Gov. DeSantis is pushing for a mid-decade redistricting session in Florida, which could target congressional districts held by Black and Hispanic Democrats.** — _Tallahassee Democrat_
---
For months, we’ve been sounding the alarm that Trump’s redistricting crisis would keep escalating. Now, Florida Governor Ron DeSantis is taking direct aim at fair representation by calling for a mid-decade map redraw that could zero in on the two districts where Black voters have the most voting power and where Hispanic Floridians support Democrats.
**Florida’s congressional map is _already_ gerrymandered. We have to be clear about what’s happening here: Ron DeSantis is moving in lockstep with Trump’s orders not only to rig the midterm elections, but also to disenfranchise voters of color by stripping them of their ability to choose leaders that represent their values.**
**The attacks on our democracy keep on coming — and we need your help to continue fighting back.[Can you make a donation of any amount to power this critical work to defend fair representation across the nation?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121533251/589761829/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_FloridaMapPoc_email_20251218-NDRC-Tier1-FloridaMapPoc_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251218-NDRC-Tier1-FloridaMapPoc_&utm_campaign=FloridaMapPoc&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjc1YzViOTM3LTJjZGMtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=w2TUPZ9rvozbayo7pRugpB92274WOqyjYFjZGG-4QlU=)**

Republicans’ effort to undermine democracy in Florida isn’t happening in a vacuum. It’s a part of a long-term strategy that’s been years in the making. And it’s happening against the backdrop of a slew of anti-voter laws passed in the last few years, which have imposed stricter voter ID laws, made voting by mail more difficult, and undermined grassroots voter registration efforts.
DeSantis has also made it clear that he’s closely watching the Supreme Court’s decision in _Louisiana v. Callais_ for a permission slip to further siphon away equal representation.
**While this news is deeply concerning for Florida voters, we must be reminded that what’s happening in Florida won’t stay in Florida. Texas, North Carolina, and Missouri show us that this is a growing threat that could reach every corner of the country.**
**[By donating to the NDRC today, you can help ensure we have the resources to keep battling to secure fair representation — whenever and wherever it comes under attack.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121533266/589761844/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_FloridaMapPoc_email_20251218-NDRC-Tier1-FloridaMapPoc_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251218-NDRC-Tier1-FloridaMapPoc_&utm_campaign=FloridaMapPoc&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjc1YzViOTM3LTJjZGMtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=w2TUPZ9rvozbayo7pRugpB92274WOqyjYFjZGG-4QlU=)**

clean_body: re: Equal representation in Florida | Peter, Fair representation for Black and Hispanic voters is under attack in Florida: | Gov. DeSantis is pushing for a mid-decade redistricting session in Florida, which could target congressional districts held by Black and Hispanic Democrats. — Tallahassee Democrat For months, we’ve been sounding the alarm that Trump’s redistricting crisis would keep escalating. Now, Florida Governor Ron DeSantis is taking direct aim at fair representation by calling for a mid-decade map redraw that could zero in on the two districts where Black voters have the most voting power and where Hispanic Floridians support Democrats. Florida’s congressional map is already gerrymandered. We have to be clear about what’s happening here: Ron DeSantis is moving in lockstep with Trump’s orders not only to rig the midterm elections, but also to disenfranchise voters of color by stripping them of their ability to choose leaders that represent their values. The attacks on our democracy keep on coming — and we need your help to continue fighting back.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Republicans’ effort to undermine democracy in Florida isn’t happening in a vacuum. It’s a part of a long-term strategy that’s been years in the making. And it’s happening against the backdrop of a slew of anti-voter laws passed in the last few years, which have imposed stricter voter ID laws, made voting by mail more difficult, and undermined grassroots voter registration efforts. DeSantis has also made it clear that he’s closely watching the Supreme Court’s decision in Louisiana v. Callais for a permission slip to further siphon away equal representation. While this news is deeply concerning for Florida voters, we must be reminded that what’s happening in Florida won’t stay in Florida. Texas, North Carolina, and Missouri show us that this is a growing threat that could reach every corner of the country. = Thank you, NDRC = = | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC. name: Jack Schlossberg
subject: I'm running for Congress!!
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=121825_1 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=121825_1]
It's official...
I'm running to represent NY-12 in Congress! This is the district where I was born and raised, and the place that five generations of my family have called home.
Hi Peter, I'm Jack Schlossberg, and my grandfather, President Kennedy, is my hero.
In high school, I spent hours watching his speeches. He taught us all that politics isn't about serving yourself — it's about serving others. And that's exactly why I'm running for Congress.
This isn't going to be your average campaign — we’re at the forefront of a movement backed by real people who are ready for a new generation of leadership. I won't be bought or intimidated. Instead, I'll be relying on folks like you. So, if you're willing to be one of the first donors to my campaign, will you split a donation between myself and Real Texas PAC right now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=121825_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jack Schlossberg and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=121825_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=121825_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=121825_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=121825_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?express_lane=true&refcode=121825_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=121825_1&amount=25]
I've always been passionate about politics — you can probably gather as much if you've seen any of my social media posts :) I pride myself on my hot (correct!) takes. But public service has always been my driving force. I come from a long legacy of public service, from my great-great-grandparents to my mother, Caroline Kennedy.
I have big shoes to fill. I've known it all my life. But this is even bigger than living up to my family name. This is about serving the people of New York — the city that shaped me into the person I am today.
Right now, our country is in crisis. Donald Trump and his buddies (my cousin RFK Jr. — to name just one) are tanking the economy, attacking free speech, and sowing distrust in decades of scientific research.
We need to revive empathy and effort. I want to be excited and feel hopeful about our future, and I want my fellow New Yorkers to feel the same way. I believe in a politics that works for the people it was intended to serve, and I'm ready to fight for it.
And Peter, I happen to think caring is cool.
None of this will be easy. But in the words of my grandfather, I'm doing this "Not because it is easy but because it is hard."
Together, we can build a new Congress and a New York City that works for everyone. Will you join our movement today by splitting a donation of any amount between my campaign and Real Texas PAC ? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jack?refcode=121825_1]
Talk soon,
Jack

name: James Carville
subject: “the strongest Democrat running statewide anywhere”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
I’m not blowing smoke when I say that the 2026 Midterm cycle is one of the most important slate of elections of my lifetime.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98610?refcode=em20251218-heartland-patriots-rob-sand&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9844%2E614464%2ElNN_mJ)
I’m not blowing smoke when I say that the 2026 Midterm cycle is one of the most important slate of elections of my lifetime.
With battlegrounds all across the country, you might be surprised to learn that I just can’t take my eyes off of Iowa. Seriously!
The _National Journal_ said Rob Sand _**“might be the strongest Democrat running statewide anywhere in the country this cycle,”**_ and listed Iowa on their list of 10 most flippable governor’s races.
My decades of experience are telling me the same thing, Peter.
**[It would be foolish to take our eye off the prize now, which is why I’m asking you to help him win this governor’s race today. Will you split a $25 donation between Heartland Patriots and Rob Sand’s campaign before his FIRST filing deadline on 12/31?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98610?refcode=em20251218-heartland-patriots-rob-sand&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9844%2E614464%2ElNN_mJ)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, your donation to Rob Sand and Heartland Patriots will process instantly:
[Contribute $25](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98611?refcode=em20251218-heartland-patriots-rob-sand&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=25&t=3&akid=9844%2E614464%2ElNN_mJ) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98610?refcode=em20251218-heartland-patriots-rob-sand&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9844%2E614464%2ElNN_mJ)
---
While the field of partisan insiders clamoring to take on Rob is particularly messy, polls show that Rob and his team are in the driver’s seat.
**_In fact, a recent poll showed Rob leading one of his possible opponents by 2 points._**
I don’t need to tell you what a big deal this is, Peter.
The partisans running the show for the last decade have underfunded public schools, endangered folks’ health care, closed rural hospitals, and watched as jobs, opportunities, and people leave Iowa to go elsewhere.
Look, we’ve been burned by Iowa before, but polling and on-the-ground sentiment is that Iowans are ready for something better.
**Rob Sand is the best candidate — _and perhaps the only one_ — who can speak to the concerns of everyday Iowans, regardless of their political leanings.**
**[But he needs YOUR help before his FIRST public fundraising deadline, Peter. clean_body: I’m not blowing smoke when I say that the 2026 Midterm cycle is one of the most important slate of elections of my lifetime. I’m not blowing smoke when I say that the 2026 Midterm cycle is one of the most important slate of elections of my lifetime. With battlegrounds all across the country, you might be surprised to learn that I just can’t take my eyes off of Iowa. Seriously! The National Journal said Rob Sand “might be the strongest Democrat running statewide anywhere in the country this cycle,” and listed Iowa on their list of 10 most flippable governor’s races. My decades of experience are telling me the same thing, Peter. If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, your donation to Rob Sand and Heartland Patriots will process instantly: While the field of partisan insiders clamoring to take on Rob is particularly messy, polls show that Rob and his team are in the driver’s seat. In fact, a recent poll showed Rob leading one of his possible opponents by 2 points. I don’t need to tell you what a big deal this is, Peter. The partisans running the show for the last decade have underfunded public schools, endangered folks’ health care, closed rural hospitals, and watched as jobs, opportunities, and people leave Iowa to go elsewhere. Look, we’ve been burned by Iowa before, but polling and on-the-ground sentiment is that Iowans are ready for something better. Rob Sand is the best candidate — and perhaps the only one — who can speak to the concerns of everyday Iowans, regardless of their political leanings.
