name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: Our district is worth fighting for.
body:
| |
---
Friend,

Heartland communities like ours have been overlooked and underinvested in for far too long. **But our district is my lifelong home, and I know it deserves better.** There's a reason I still live here, friend, in the same house where I grew up. It's because our district — and our community — are worth fighting for. I'm working every day to deliver good-paying jobs, lower costs, and transformative investments for Northwest Ohio. I'm fighting for a cleaner Great Lakes ecosystem and affordable healthcare for every American. I'm fighting for the working people who have been struggling against an unfair system for far too long. The people who worked with my mom at Champion Spark Plug. The neighbors who help grow tomatoes in the garden I planted in my yard. The Ohioans who build the products that keep our country running, and who grow the foods that keep our country fed. I'll never stop fighting for you, friend. That's why the special interest groups are spending tens of millions of dollars to defeat me. **Our district is one of the most vulnerable blue seats in the nation. It's key to taking back the House in 2026. It's a district that's worth fighting for.** **[Will you please chip in any amount to power our work and defend this frontline seat, friend?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121550992/589819556/-1968428927?refcode=251218_mk_reIntro_l1&ms=251218_mk_reIntro&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4Njk3YTk4My00ZmRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bWv9Eaoj-QmP5aXjvEkykHvu30Cb3xC9c7ixom-v-Dw=)** | [Give $25 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121550999/589819563/1039792322?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=251218_mk_reIntro&ms=251218_mk_reIntro&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4Njk3YTk4My00ZmRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bWv9Eaoj-QmP5aXjvEkykHvu30Cb3xC9c7ixom-v-Dw=) | [Give $50 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121551000/589819564/1039792322?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=251218_mk_reIntro&ms=251218_mk_reIntro&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4Njk3YTk4My00ZmRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bWv9Eaoj-QmP5aXjvEkykHvu30Cb3xC9c7ixom-v-Dw=)

### About Marcy Kaptur
Marcy Kaptur is a fighter for high-wage jobs, growing a workforce to meet the needs of the 21st century, and protecting our region’s most vital natural resource, the Great Lakes. She’s worked with both parties to secure investments in our communities and has been a true friend of veterans, seniors, first responders and working people. clean_body: | | Friend, Heartland communities like ours have been overlooked and underinvested in for far too long. But our district is my lifelong home, and I know it deserves better. There's a reason I still live here, friend, in the same house where I grew up. It's because our district — and our community — are worth fighting for. I'm working every day to deliver good-paying jobs, lower costs, and transformative investments for Northwest Ohio. I'm fighting for a cleaner Great Lakes ecosystem and affordable healthcare for every American. I'm fighting for the working people who have been struggling against an unfair system for far too long. The people who worked with my mom at Champion Spark Plug. The neighbors who help grow tomatoes in the garden I planted in my yard. The Ohioans who build the products that keep our country running, and who grow the foods that keep our country fed. I'll never stop fighting for you, friend. That's why the special interest groups are spending tens of millions of dollars to defeat me. Our district is one of the most vulnerable blue seats in the nation. It's key to taking back the House in 2026. It's a district that's worth fighting for. = | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately. Thank you so much, Marcy | | | ---|---|--- About Marcy Kaptur Marcy Kaptur is a fighter for high-wage jobs, growing a workforce to meet the needs of the 21st century, and protecting our region’s most vital natural resource, the Great Lakes. name: Sen. Jeff Bridges
subject: It's déjà vu all over again with TABOR
body:
It’s feeling a bit like Groundhog Day at the Capitol. The Joint Budget Committee has started meeting again—and it's going to be

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/dfa696fed23f/get-more-smarter-on-colorados-budget-10372142?e=b48bb2b4f4)
---
[|
---](https://bridgesforcolorado.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ba078768558df9fe7d8559d49&id=8790aee86b&e=b48bb2b4f4)
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Hi there,
It’s feeling a bit like Groundhog Day at the Capitol. The Joint Budget Committee has started meeting again—and it's going to be another rough year.
Last year, when I chaired the committee, we cut more than a billion dollars because of the rationing cap in TABOR. Yet we still managed to pull a rabbit out of the hat and actually increase funding for public schools, public safety, public lands, and Medicaid. Unfortunately, there are no more rabbits left in that hat.
---
[|
---](https://bridgesforcolorado.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ba078768558df9fe7d8559d49&id=0ba190343e&e=b48bb2b4f4)
| |

As we head into 2026, Colorado once again has a budget shortfall and a budget surplus all at the same time. We’re cutting public services while also issuing TABOR refunds. That combination isn’t a fluke. It’s the predictable result of how TABOR works.

TABOR has a rationing cap that only lets our budget grow by inflation plus population. That may sound reasonable—just do the same thing every year!—except that our costs grow faster than inflation. Medical costs rise faster than inflation, and Medicaid alone makes up about a third of the state budget. Wages also tend to outpace inflation, and about 90 percent of K–12 funding goes to salary for educators and staff—same for prisons, another major part of our budget. And then there's the rising cost of construction materials for roads and bridges...
This means that even in years when revenues are strong and the budget technically grows, we still have to cut roughly a billion dollars in real services just to get to balance. And we'll have to do it every single year from now until forever unless we change TABOR.
**This isn’t waste or mismanagement. It's rules written in the 1990s colliding with the real cost of running a modern state.**
Budgets can feel abstract, but when they don’t work people feel it in classrooms, clinics, and communities across Colorado. I’m working closely with the Colorado Education Association on a potential fix to TABOR, but there's much more work to do and details to work out. Stay tuned!

**Addressing the challenges of TABOR is a major part of why I'm running for Treasurer. We're just $3,000 away from raising $100k this quarter. That's a huge number for a State Treasurer's race. If you can, I would deeply appreciate any support you can give to hit our goal.**
---
| [CLICK TO DONATE](https://bridgesforcolorado.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ba078768558df9fe7d8559d49&id=73995a14d9&e=b48bb2b4f4)
---
| | Talk soon,
P.S. Kit got to run through snow for the first time ever! He’ll refuse to wear mittens and then lose it when his hands get cold, but he’s a born outdoorsman.
---
|
---
| | | | | [](https://bridgesforcolorado.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ba078768558df9fe7d8559d49&id=8870ffe1b2&e=b48bb2b4f4)
---
[](https://bridgesforcolorado.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ba078768558df9fe7d8559d49&id=a993bcd645&e=b48bb2b4f4)

---

body:
|
#
---
Peter — we need to talk about Tina’s opponent, Christine Drazan. Drazan ran and lost against Tina last cycle, but if you need a refresher, here’s a reminder of her terrible track record:
* Sided with Donald Trump on deploying the National Guard to Portland
* Supports a dangerous abortion ban that would take away a woman’s right to choose
* Voted to cut funding for Medicaid, threatening health care for 1.3 million Oregonians
* Wants to eliminate Oregon’s vote-by-mail system, a model of secure, accessible elections

Drazan has been attacking Tina left and right since launching her campaign for Governor, saying Tina’s been _“focused on Pennsylvania Avenue, not Main Street.”_ That’s pretty rich, considering Drazan is lock-step with Donald Trump and the far-right. **Meanwhile, Tina remains laser-focused on the job at hand — solving actual problems for the people of Oregon. name: Mark Kelly
subject: I will not give up on our country.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
It has given me too much, and I'll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
| | | Folks,

If you can believe it, things didn't stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn't stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. It didn't stop when the FBI wanted to talk to me and other members of Congress. Because now the Department of Defense has announced that it is escalating its investigation into me.

All for saying things the president didn't like to hear.

If you're feeling like this isn't the country you grew up in, I don't blame you. But I hope you aren't giving up on it, folks.

I certainly haven't. This country has given me too much, and I'll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.

There's no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None.

If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he's got another thing coming.

And if you'd be willing to help support me and the DSCC as we gear up to fight back against Donald Trump and his Administration by taking back the Senate majority, please:

[**Split a donation between the DSCC and me. It would mean a lot.**](https://go.dscc.org/go/22331?amount=5&t=4&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FM-20251218-MarkKelly-KellyIWillNotGiveUpTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20251218-MarkKelly-KellyIWillNotGiveUpTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=22161%2E9716520%2EGmMKZ_)

name: Cori Bush
subject: They’re scared of us, and they should be
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori For Us
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251218_redistricting_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251218_redistricting_act]
The newly enacted gerrymandered congressional maps in Missouri are the GOP’s latest attack on our voting rights.The people didn’t ask for a new map, but we know the GOP rarely works in the interest of the people.
It really just shows how far they will go to try to suppress our votes. They’re scared of Missouri voters, scared of communities that show up for each other, and scared of leaders who fight for the people instead of bowing to the wealthy and well-connected.
In Congress, I fought to expand voting rights, protect our democracy, and push back against extremists. When I’m back in D.C., you better believe we will hold MAGA Republicans accountable for trying to rig the system, dilute our voice, and put partisan power over the people.
This fight isn’t just about lines on a map; it’s about our right to choose our leaders and our future. Extremists will stop at nothing to grab power, but we are not backing down. We’re showing up, organizing, and fighting for fair representation. They should be very afraid of our movement, Peter.
Let’s show them real people-power.
Chip in $5, [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251218_redistricting_act] $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251118_heart_act] , or $25 today to help us fight back, defend our democracy, and keep our voices strong in Missouri. Every vote matters on election day, and every dollar matters in this fight against the forces trying to buy elections and silence us. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251218_redistricting_act]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

In solidarity,
— Cori
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251218_redistricting_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251218_redistricting_act] Cori Bush is a pastor, nurse, survivor, and formerly unhoused single mother of two who knows what it means to struggle and what it means to rise .
As a Congresswoman, she has always shown up when it’s hardest: securing disaster relief, lowering insulin costs, and delivering millions for our communities.
Across St. Louis, families are being priced out and pushed out. Cori Bush is fighting back for affordable housing, fair wages, and utilities as a human right. Be part of this people-first movement. Visit CoriBush.org [https://www.coribush.org] to learn more.

##### _This post is sponsored by NordProtect._
* * *
_**by Cliff Schecter**_
After a seemingly never-ending weekend of horrific news, from the mass murder of Jews celebrating Hanukkah in Australia to a mass shooting at Brown University back here in America, I searched for the words to convey what I--what many of us--was feeling.
Eventually I conjured up syllables and phrases, best I could, to respond to a third shock-to-the-system--during the _two-day respite we call a weekend, because it 's supposed to be when the week ends. _But in this world it's no longer the case _._ And so it was, with the tragic murders of Rob and Michele Singer Reiner.
[| | | Cliff Schecter 3dThe Rob Reiner news is obviously heartbreaking. I can honestly say "A Few Good Men," "The American President," & "The Princess Bride" are 3 of my fave films. That's not even getting into the hilarity of "This Is Spinal Tap." But he and Michele Singer Reiner were so much more. They were our collective conscience outraged and vocal about our profoundly damaged values and a broken system. One in which authoritarians run...
---
| 113| | 23|
---|---|---|---](https://substack.com/profile/31941177-cliff-schecter/note/c-188018583)
As I pondered the larger mysteries of why this happens, and whether our species is made to make it or just some kinda cosmic accident, I thought about his films.
Then it hit me. These jeremiads from his films I quoted in college with some drink in me, as my Brit friends might say, provided two of the most important monologues to explain our present-day crisis.
A yin AND a yang, if you will. Let me explain.
* * *
Blue Amp Media is reader-supported--subscribe for free!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.gvIEmDisjMEPbDunQ26TVQNHSRxGDA2f5Xdcm6d5ryU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=182025421)
* * *
### You Can't Handle The Truth!
[As we illegally bomb Caribbean-based boats](https://substack.com/redirect/9af22e4c-65ae-4f1e-b5e2-e50e5bcd95ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), hijack tankers and blockade other ships, seemingly preparing for the stupidest, most counterproductive land war since the last one Republicans tricked us into (we were reminded to "never get into a land war in Asia" in another classic Reiner film, _The Princess Bride._ I'm quite sure that goes for South America too) I thought about Colonel Nathan Jessup from _A Few Good Men_.
The penultimate trial scene is one of the more famous in cinema, as Jack Nicholson's performance oozes arrogance and contempt for the "elites" _who don 't get our world_, the way he of course does. You can feel him spitting hubris from the other side of the camera. I'd call it very J.D. Vance or Peter Hegseth--except Jessup doesn't have the charisma of a cadaver (Vance) not possess the brain power of a bird (Hegseth).
The parallels to **now** almost make it feel like Reiner was preparing us for this moment. Jessup was based in Guantanamo Bay--which is where Hegseth deployed as a "platoon leader for security" from 2004-2005.
Considering it was a black hole for detainee dignity, it's likely where Hegseth developed a taste for cruelty and violations of international law, just as he did for Johnny Walker. And Jim Beam. And Jack Daniels. [Luckily no "Charlie Kirk Wine" (no, I'm not kidding, it's real](https://substack.com/redirect/b83e9861-fbd5-4944-9890-bd05e1430486?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)).
I don't think Reiner, who produced the film, and writer Aaron Sorkin, could've nailed our present dystopia better than a locating the central part of the plot on the base in Gitmo that would become a post-9/11 gulag, now used to process "migrants." And have a central theme of this masterpiece be about torture committed by our men, with the question asked and answered: _are they required to ignore illegal orders from fascistic commanders?_
_So we 're off, with that epic moment from 1992's A Few Good Men:_
**LTJG Kaffee:** Colonel Jessup! Did you order the Code Red?!
**Judge Randolph:** You don't have to answer that question!
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Col Jessup:** I'll answer the question. You want answers?
**LTJG Kaffee:** I think I'm entitled to them.
**Col Jessup:** You want answers!?!
**LTJG Kaffee:** I want the truth!
**Col Jessup:** You can't handle the truth!!
Son, we live in a world that has walls, and those walls have to be guarded by men with guns. Who's gonna do it? You? You, Lieutenant Weinberg? I have a greater responsibility than you can _possibly_ fathom. You weep for Santiago, and you curse the Marines. You have that luxury. You have the luxury of not knowing what I know -- that Santiago's death, while tragic, probably saved lives; and my existence, while grotesque and incomprehensible to you, saves lives.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
You don't want the truth because deep down in places you don't talk about at parties, you want me on that wall -- you _need_ me on that wall.
We use words like "honor," "code," "loyalty." We use these words as the backbone of a life spent defending something. You use them as a punch line.
I have neither the time nor the inclination to explain myself to a man who rises and sleeps under the blanket of the very freedom that I provide and then questions the manner in which I provide it.
I would rather that you just said "thank you" and went on your way. Otherwise, I suggest you pick up a weapon and stand the post. Either way, I don't give a DAMN what you think you're entitled to!
**LTJG Kaffee:** Did you order the Code Red?
**Col Jessup:** I did the job --
**LTJG Kaffee:** \-- Did you order the Code Red?!
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Col Jessup:** YOU'RE GOD DAMN RIGHT I DID!!!
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
I can never fully express my appreciation for the men and women who take the ultimate risk guarding those walls. But Jessup's protestation of having "neither the time nor inclination to explain" himself to a prosecutor and/or judge in a courtroom?
Sorry pal, that ain't it, not close to constitutionally acceptable. Which is why it wasn't until Hegseth, Noem, Homan, Patel, Bondi and the rest of the broken-toy, beating-heart-of-darkness-bitch brigade ascended into positions of power that the elected and appointed civilian stewards of our democracy began to be flipped off like they were Donald Trump...anywhere.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
When Jessup says "I'd prefer a simple thank you and be on my way" and then adds his primal scream, "I don't give a damn what you think you're entitled to," this is exactly what Hegseth now wants. Reporters get banned from the Pentagon for not turning in their questions ahead of time. Congressional subpoenas, questions and court orders are ignored.
[There's a name for this: ](https://substack.com/redirect/d3d086e4-0300-49a5-8393-30f636cdd226?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[Dictatorship.](https://substack.com/redirect/d3d086e4-0300-49a5-8393-30f636cdd226?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **_Even back in 1992, Rob Reiner saw it coming._
We're a democratic republic. We have a Congress--[even if its "Speaker" is an impotent cheese doodle who's prostrated himself before Trump](https://substack.com/redirect/4d5b608f-17ab-4d66-9181-bbf2069fd996?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and spends his time monitoring his son's porn. Meaning: this isn't your call, Pete-o.
You damn well fucking better _take the time and have the inclination to explain yourself_ to the people's elected representatives and our judicial branch. That's how _**it works**_. Or maybe you'll find yourself on the other side of those Gitmo bars at some point.
I suppose it isn't a complete surprise that Reiner and Sorkin saw all this in 1992, not long after the very-obviously-guilty perpetrators of Iran-Contra walked away scot-free, and we engaged in whatever the hell Panama and Grenada were, other than polling boosts for _Bedtime with Bonzo_.
Past is inevitably prologue if not punished.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
### I was so busy keeping my job, I forgot to do my job
Has a phrase ever been more apt for too many Democrats and the leadership of the party as a fascist force tries to end our democratic experiment? Those simply _refusing_ to craft a simple narrative for[ how a herd of sick billionaires and their DC butlers jointly robbed us of our rights and financial security](https://substack.com/redirect/d3d086e4-0300-49a5-8393-30f636cdd226?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by directly assaulting our democracy and economy--which go hand-in-hand.
[Supporting this simple ](https://substack.com/redirect/92665ae2-b321-4a5d-8dda-e123b2ac7c02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[and true](https://substack.com/redirect/92665ae2-b321-4a5d-8dda-e123b2ac7c02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ narrative with a emotionally and culturally resonant messages](https://substack.com/redirect/92665ae2-b321-4a5d-8dda-e123b2ac7c02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and undergirding them with wholesale policy changes we'll make when we sweep their venal asses out of office in '26, should be a top priority. It should be tied to stories from our past, and part of an argument _not to return to the America we were_ , but _our ability to overcome any obstacle to become the America we 've always sought to be_.
President Andy Shepherd (Michael Douglas) gives the speech below from _The American President,_ when he finally breaks away from from a DC-political-consultant mentality, or the Schumer-Jeffries mode of caution, caving and small-bore change.
In the film, President Shepherd shows all the behaviors that drive us nuts--a lack of willingness to hit back when attacked, go on offense, create a narrative about his opponent, or go big on policy. He allows this oleaginous, overbearing d*k-in-a-box, (looks like Mitch McConnell, sounds like Ted Cruz) right-wing crank, Senator Rumson, to attack him using what else, but GOP-culture-war garbage.
Shepherd's aides vociferously encourage him to respond. But he--like Democratic leaders looking at polling on crime and immigration and cowering instead of countering--thinks what people believe is sacrosanct. Who is he to argue with them? _It 's not like you can persuade people and change poll numbers, right?_
Finally, President Shepherd gets it, catches a whiff of the verbal beatdown he must dropship on Rumson, and just comes out firing, messaging, counter-messaging, and fully takes apart his Ultra-MAGA opponent _in the exact way Democrats should every day_ :
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Reporter:** Robyn, will the President ever respond to Senator Rumson's question about being a member of the American Civil Liberties Union?
**President Shepherd:** Yes, he will. Good morning. [Members of the White House Press Corps begin to rise] It's alright. Please keep your seats. Good morning.
For the last couple of months, Senator Rumson has suggested that being President of this country was, to a certain extent, about character. And although I've not been willing to engage in his attacks on me, I have been here three years and three days, and I can tell you without hesitation: Being President of this country is entirely about character.
* * *
Subscribe to BAM for free!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.gvIEmDisjMEPbDunQ26TVQNHSRxGDA2f5Xdcm6d5ryU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=182025421)
* * *
For the record, yes, I am a card-carrying member of the ACLU, but the more important question is "Why aren't you, Bob?" Now this is an organization whose sole purpose is to defend the Bill of Rights, so it naturally begs the question, why would a senator, his party's most powerful spokesman and a candidate for President, choose to reject upholding the constitution? Now if you can answer that question, folks, then you're smarter than I am, because I didn't understand it until a few hours ago.
America isn't easy. America is advanced citizenship. You've gotta want it bad, 'cause it's gonna put up a fight. It's gonna say, "You want free speech? Let's see you acknowledge a man whose words make your blood boil, who's standing center stage and advocating at the top of his lungs that which you would spend a lifetime opposing at the top of yours."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
You want to claim this land as the land of the free? Then the symbol of your country cannot just be a flag. The symbol also has to be one of its citizens exercising his right to burn that flag in protest. Now show me that, defend that, celebrate that in your classrooms.
Then you can stand up and sing about the land of the free.
I've known Bob Rumson for years. And I've been operating under the assumption that the reason Bob devotes so much time and energy to shouting at the rain was that he simply didn't get it. Well, I was wrong. Bob's problem isn't that he doesn't get it. Bob's problem is that he can't sell it!
We have serious problems to solve, and we need serious people to solve them. And whatever your particular problem is, I promise you Bob Rumson is not the least bit interested in solving it. He is interested in two things, and two things only: making you afraid of it, and telling you who's to blame for it. That, ladies and gentlemen, is how you win elections.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
You gather a group of middle age, middle class, middle income voters who remember with longing an easier time, and you talk to them about family, and American values and character, and you wave an old photo of the President's girlfriend and you scream about patriotism. You tell them she's to blame for their lot in life. And you go on television and you call her a whore.
Sydney Ellen Wade has done nothing to you, Bob. She has done nothing but put herself through school, represent the interests of public school teachers, and lobby for the safety of our natural resources. You want a character debate, Bob? You better stick with me, 'cause Sydney Ellen Wade is way out of your league.
I've loved two women in my life. I lost one to cancer. And I lost the other 'cause I was so busy keeping my job, I forgot to do my job. Well, that ends right now.
Tomorrow morning the White House is sending a bill to Congress for its consideration. It's White House Resolution 455, an energy bill requiring a twenty percent reduction of the emission of fossil fuels over the next ten years. It is by far the most aggressive stride ever taken in the fight to reverse the effects of global warming.
The other piece of legislation is the crime bill. As of today, it no longer exists. I'm throwing it out. I'm throwing it out and writing a law that makes sense. You cannot address crime prevention without getting rid of assault weapons and hand guns. I consider them a threat to national security, and I will go door to door if I have to, but I'm gonna convince Americans that I'm right, and I'm gonna get the guns.
We've got serious problems, and we need serious people. And if you want to talk about character, Bob, you'd better come at me with more than a burning flag and a membership card. If you want to talk about character and American values, fine. Just tell me where and when, and I'll show up. This a time for serious people, Bob, and your fifteen minutes are up.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
My name is Andrew Shepherd, and I AM the President.
* * *
Reiner gave us both of these gifts 30 years ago. Perhaps, as he rests in peace after a life of service and accomplishment, we can accept them now. RIP Rob and Michele Singer Reiner. Let's honor all that they were by implementing what they were telling us in these two all-time great movie monologues, and take our damn democracy back.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2c9bcdf7-07c9-4253-911a-65c82b959309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

The producer, actor, writer, philanthropist, and political activist will be greatly missed, but he left us a parting gift, if we'll accept it | | and | Dec 18 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- This post is sponsored by NordProtect. by Cliff Schecter After a seemingly never-ending weekend of horrific news, from the mass murder of Jews celebrating Hanukkah in Australia to a mass shooting at Brown University back here in America, I searched for the words to convey what I--what many of us--was feeling. Eventually I conjured up syllables and phrases, best I could, to respond to a third shock-to-the-system--during the two-day respite we call a weekend, because it 's supposed to be when the week ends. But in this world it's no longer the case . And so it was, with the tragic murders of Rob and Michele Singer Reiner. As I pondered the larger mysteries of why this happens, and whether our species is made to make it or just some kinda cosmic accident, I thought about his films. Then it hit me. These jeremiads from his films I quoted in college with some drink in me, as my Brit friends might say, provided two of the most important monologues to explain our present-day crisis. A yin AND a yang, if you will. Let me explain. Blue Amp Media is reader-supported--subscribe for free! You Can't Handle The Truth! , hijack tankers and blockade other ships, seemingly preparing for the stupidest, most counterproductive land war since the last one Republicans tricked us into (we were reminded to "never get into a land war in Asia" in another classic Reiner film, The Princess Bride. I'm quite sure that goes for South America too) I thought about Colonel Nathan Jessup from A Few Good Men. The penultimate trial scene is one of the more famous in cinema, as Jack Nicholson's performance oozes arrogance and contempt for the "elites" who don 't get our world, the way he of course does. You can feel him spitting hubris from the other side of the camera. I'd call it very J.D. Vance or Peter Hegseth--except Jessup doesn't have the charisma of a cadaver (Vance) not possess the brain power of a bird (Hegseth). The parallels to now almost make it feel like Reiner was preparing us for this moment. Jessup was based in Guantanamo Bay--which is where Hegseth deployed as a "platoon leader for security" from 2004-2005. Considering it was a black hole for detainee dignity, it's likely where Hegseth developed a taste for cruelty and violations of international law, just as he did for Johnny Walker. And Jim Beam. And Jack Daniels. ). I don't think Reiner, who produced the film, and writer Aaron Sorkin, could've nailed our present dystopia better than a locating the central part of the plot on the base in Gitmo that would become a post-9/11 gulag, now used to process "migrants." And have a central theme of this masterpiece be about torture committed by our men, with the question asked and answered: are they required to ignore illegal orders from fascistic commanders? So we 're off, with that epic moment from 1992's A Few Good Men: LTJG Kaffee: Colonel Jessup! Did you order the Code Red?! Judge Randolph: You don't have to answer that question! | | ---|---|--- Col Jessup: I'll answer the question. You want answers? LTJG Kaffee: I think I'm entitled to them. Col Jessup: You want answers!?! LTJG Kaffee: I want the truth! Col Jessup: You can't handle the truth!! Son, we live in a world that has walls, and those walls have to be guarded by men with guns. Who's gonna do it? You? You, Lieutenant Weinberg? I have a greater responsibility than you can possibly fathom. You weep for Santiago, and you curse the Marines. You have that luxury. You have the luxury of not knowing what I know -- that Santiago's death, while tragic, probably saved lives; and my existence, while grotesque and incomprehensible to you, saves lives. | | ---|---|--- You don't want the truth because deep down in places you don't talk about at parties, you want me on that wall -- you need me on that wall. We use words like "honor," "code," "loyalty." We use these words as the backbone of a life spent defending something. You use them as a punch line. I have neither the time nor the inclination to explain myself to a man who rises and sleeps under the blanket of the very freedom that I provide and then questions the manner in which I provide it. I would rather that you just said "thank you" and went on your way. Otherwise, I suggest you pick up a weapon and stand the post. Either way, I don't give a DAMN what you think you're entitled to! LTJG Kaffee: Did you order the Code Red? Col Jessup: I did the job -- LTJG Kaffee: \-- Did you order the Code Red?! | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- Col Jessup: YOU'RE GOD DAMN RIGHT I DID!!! | | ---|---|--- I can never fully express my appreciation for the men and women who take the ultimate risk guarding those walls. But Jessup's protestation of having "neither the time nor inclination to explain" himself to a prosecutor and/or judge in a courtroom? Sorry pal, that ain't it, not close to constitutionally acceptable. Which is why it wasn't until Hegseth, Noem, Homan, Patel, Bondi and the rest of the broken-toy, beating-heart-of-darkness-bitch brigade ascended into positions of power that the elected and appointed civilian stewards of our democracy began to be flipped off like they were Donald Trump...anywhere. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- When Jessup says "I'd prefer a simple thank you and be on my way" and then adds his primal scream, "I don't give a damn what you think you're entitled to," this is exactly what Hegseth now wants. Reporters get banned from the Pentagon for not turning in their questions ahead of time. Congressional subpoenas, questions and court orders are ignored. Even back in 1992, Rob Reiner saw it coming. We're a democratic republic. We have a Congress-- and spends his time monitoring his son's porn. Meaning: this isn't your call, Pete-o. name: Stasha Rhodes, United for Democracy
subject: Re: They rewrote American democracy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United for Democracy
body:
The Supreme Court went full MAGA this year—and they’ll only get worse in 2026. | |
---
Peter, **The cases the Supreme Court ruled on this year will have toxic effects on Americans for decades to come—and the hits will only keep coming in 2026.** From giving a wannabe dictator more kingly powers, to consolidating power and influence for the wealthy, to attacking the voting rights of everyday people, the MAGA justices are rewriting our constitution to Trump’s liking. [**We have to fight back, and quickly. Chip in today to power our fight to keep corruption at the Supreme Court front and center as we fight to undo the damage they did this term.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvvXHLjFB30oSPp5L_UbTHq66ariRDe7RRbLMlyGm74UUETKjnTMukpe2oXWnpOLuBk4oUlzbgj_TK6lLEkRmXGBuSiZ4rjXmk4ia1PZz5hp_CkRSyf0UQ-lfZKjzflQo57D3dGbrwomKapHKW79N1BVL0a2SLs-qLxBudVpPqCcijvW3KuooY-oc7oEPcJAjFw2HxY9cdbyI3bxNA9E7bFigkI_hhUnlQE8qpNXkymrlBtVHxX55uiCtxriWi2Q7HRy1hDlKDnm9O1Me-M6bXX5y3lcdCjRcNPVx-EJrvCV8sKHvKH-1fldyW18to190IFT1y6vEOcF2Ps6VSPj3gBs9UUnrhGdsmPpuYOL7HAZQ/4mj/tmnkK7TFTBq-bMZp3F7mNQ/h0/h001.It1BgWYo19r-4Q4x_kOeem_JcBNG0Y4uva6jEDD_MPI) Thank you for supporting democracy over a wannabe dictator, Stasha Rhodes
United for Democracy **\-- PREVIOUS EMAIL --** Peter, **2025 will be remembered as the year the Supreme Court went full MAGA.** **This year, the Court didn’t call balls and strikes—it completely rewrote America’s democracy.** In an effort to prop up a wannabe dictator, the MAGA justices tilted the balance of power away from the people and toward executive power and corporate interests. In case after case, the Court chose power over the people. They threw trans kids and immigrants under the bus, placed an even bigger crown on Donald Trump’s head, and tore apart years of progress toward a fair and just democracy. Now, the MAGA justices are poised to issue even more toxic rulings in cases they’ve heard recently. **Five cases in particular have the potential to reap enormous effects on our democracy for decades to come:**
* The Court heard **_Trump v. Slaughter_** this month,**__** and signaled they were likely to give Trump the power to fire the heads of independent agencies, overturning 90 years of precedent and consolidating even more executive control.
* The Court also heard **_NRSC v. FEC,_** a case which could give them the ability to strike down limits on how much political parties can spend in coordination with candidates, giving big, billionaire money even more power over our democracy.

* The Court has agreed to hear Trump’s case on **birthright citizenship.** Even deciding to take the case is historically significant—the Court could decide to rip apart 127 years of precedent and end the constitutional guarantee that if you’re born on American soil, you’re an American citizen.
* In an emergency order, the Court blessed **Texas’ racially gerrymandered Congressional maps,****__** diluting Black and Latino voting power, attacking the Voting Rights Act, and giving Republicans the partisan edge in next year’s midterm elections.
* The Court is also taking up **_Louisiana v. Callais,_** a case that could rip apart the Voting Rights Act entirely and unleash partisan, racial gerrymandering across the country.

**America is going to feel the pain from their rulings for decades, and it’s going to take a lot of time and work to right the justices’ wrongs.** Presidential power is expanding unchecked, voting rights are under siege, and the Court is siding with moneyed interests over everyday Americans. But Supreme Court justices aren’t the only ones who get to decide what the Constitution means and what our democracy should be. **That’s why we’re building a network of experts, activists, and organizations dedicated to ending the MAGA corruption of the Supreme Court and taking back our democracy.** [**United for Democracy produced expert analysis, disseminated social media and talking points, and rallied activists every time the Supreme Court ruled against We the People this year. name: Jazz Lewis
subject: Cancelled: January 8 Annapolis Event
body:
https://www.jazzlewis.com/ [https://www.jazzlewis.com/]
Dear Friend,
Yesterday, I announced that I will be resigning from the House of Delegates and transitioning to a new role at University of Maryland. With that, my event planned for January 8th at Federal House in Annapolis has been cancelled.
As always, I appreciate the support extended to me over the past decade and look forward to seeing you around.
Please contact MeaghanElsner@RiceConsultingLLC.com [MeaghanElsner@RiceConsultingLLC.com] or call 410-838-6355 for any assistance or questions.
In service,
Jazz

P.O. Box 341854
Bethesda, MD 20827
United States

name: Chip Roy
subject: Downright terrible
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TEXANS FOR CHIP ROY
body:
Texans deserve better than whatever it is Jasmine Crockett is offering |
Fellow Patriot,

The Left loves to believe they're superior in every way to Republicans.

They think they're smarter ...
They think they're more successful ...
They believe they're morally superior ...

But I can't say that any of that is _actually_ true.

I can't think of a single Republican I know who would make fun of Texas Governor Greg Abbot's use of a wheelchair - but I know one Democrat who has, my House colleague, Jasmine Crockett, the Left's newest darling, who is running for the U.S. Senate in Texas.

This woman is the personification of Trump Derangement Syndrome.

She's focused on woke racism.

She wants to defund the police.

She wants reparations for slavery and doesn't believe in border control.

Jasmine Crockett has even gone so far as to say that Democrats should "beat down" Republicans.

This woman isn't fit to serve the state she's from - neither in the House nor the Senate.

I need your help to stop the worst from happening before it's too late. With more than 3 million followers on social media, this radical leftist will surely be raking in the cash - and probably is as I write this.

Even if you're not from Texas, you know the state is Republicans' crown jewel on the electoral map, and we can't for a moment cede any control to a Bernie Sanders Democrat.

clean_body: Texans deserve better than whatever it is Jasmine Crockett is offering | Fellow Patriot, The Left loves to believe they're superior in every way to Republicans. They think they're smarter ... They think they're more successful ... They believe they're morally superior ... But I can't say that any of that is actually true. I can't think of a single Republican I know who would make fun of Texas Governor Greg Abbot's use of a wheelchair - but I know one Democrat who has, my House colleague, Jasmine Crockett, the Left's newest darling, who is running for the U.S. Senate in Texas. This woman is the personification of Trump Derangement Syndrome. She's focused on woke racism. She wants to defund the police. She wants reparations for slavery and doesn't believe in border control. Jasmine Crockett has even gone so far as to say that Democrats should "beat down" Republicans. This woman isn't fit to serve the state she's from - neither in the House nor the Senate. I need your help to stop the worst from happening before it's too late. With more than 3 million followers on social media, this radical leftist will surely be raking in the cash - and probably is as I write this. Even if you're not from Texas, you know the state is Republicans' crown jewel on the electoral map, and we can't for a moment cede any control to a Bernie Sanders Democrat. With gratitude, Chip Roy | | PAID FOR BY TEXANS FOR CHIP ROY Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but .

| | | **Dry Eyes? The Problem Isn’t on the Surface**
If you’re constantly reaching for eye drops, you’re not alone—but you might be treating the _wrong problem_.
Most Americans are told to “just use drops,” yet chronic dry eye often begins inside the body—rooted in inflammation and a lack of critical omega-3s. That’s why drops offer a few minutes of comfort... and then the burning, itching, and irritation return.
What actually makes a difference? Not generic fish oil—your body can’t absorb enough of it. The key is **high-absorption omega-3s paired with the antioxidant astaxanthin** , shown to support natural tear production, calm irritation, and protect delicate eye tissue.
It’s no wonder thousands of Americans are finally finding real, lasting relief—without living bottle-to-bottle on eye drops.
If you’re tired of temporary fixes, this short report explains the truth behind dry eye—and the solution that’s helping so many people:
[👉](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e96808/VaHR0cHM6Ly9mYi5uYXRpdmVwYXRoLmNvbS9yZC9yLnBocA/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNTEyMTg/LbmNsa05ZdWRueg/qP3NpZD0xNzE0JnB1Yj0yNDA1NzMmYzE9MjAyNTEyMTctRlRELTAmYzI9QUlERSZjMz1GVEQ/gaURqDw/sauf39f6147) **[5 Reasons Drops Won’t Work (and What Actually Does)](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e96808/VaHR0cHM6Ly9mYi5uYXRpdmVwYXRoLmNvbS9yZC9yLnBocA/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNTEyMTg/LbmNsa05ZdWRueg/qP3NpZD0xNzE0JnB1Yj0yNDA1NzMmYzE9MjAyNTEyMTctRlRELTAmYzI9QUlERSZjMz1GVEQ/gaURqDw/sauf39f6147)**

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Neil Armstrong reported a strange encounter he had in space during a post-mission debrief.| | | | December 18 | | | | | What Neil Armstrong REALLY saw on the moon [TERRIFYING] | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. It’s been a big year.

In the Senate, we’ve delivered President Trump’s agenda, cut your taxes, and secured our border.

name: Becca Balint
subject: May this be the LAST time…

body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121552507/589824529/-785282667?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYWVkZWI2YS01NGRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_-uUZ_gLw-Mm0vrvZdw58eoQALT6sz7ykEdICoI035s=)

Peter, _“May this be the last time America elects a president devoid of sympathy and compassion.”_ **I said those words on the House floor this week because I meant them.** Cruelty from our leaders is not strength. Mocking grief is not leadership. And stripping people of their dignity — especially in moments of loss — is not who we should be as a country. Leadership matters. Character matters. And the way we talk about people matters. [**If you believe our country deserves leaders who lead with decency and compassion, I hope you’ll consider clicking here and chipping in today. Even $5 helps us go into this election year strong, organized, and ready to win.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121552509/589824532/-892689124?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYWVkZWI2YS01NGRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_-uUZ_gLw-Mm0vrvZdw58eoQALT6sz7ykEdICoI035s=) **[CHIP IN TODAY HERE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121552511/589824534/-892689124?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYWVkZWI2YS01NGRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_-uUZ_gLw-Mm0vrvZdw58eoQALT6sz7ykEdICoI035s=)** I was raised to believe you don’t punch down. You don’t make fun of people. You don’t divide for the sake of division. And you don’t treat cruelty like it’s a virtue. **I’m in Congress to fight for something better.** A country where people can afford a place to live — especially in my home state of Vermont, where the housing crisis is squeezing families and seniors alike. A country where people can get care when they need it, including mental health care, without fear or stigma. A country where corporations don’t get to rig the system while working people are left behind. A country where democracy, due process, and our freedoms are protected — no matter who’s in power. **And if you believe — _like I do_ — that this should be the last time we accept cruelty from our leaders, I need you in this fight with me today.** As we head into a new year, we also head into a moment where real change is possible. A year where we can start building a government that’s rooted in decency, fairness, and respect — and where leaders are held to a higher standard. [**If you’re ready to help elect leaders who fight for people, protect our democracy, and lead with compassion, I hope you’ll consider chipping in today. Whether it’s $5, $25, or whatever feels right, your support helps us keep moving forward together.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121552513/589824536/-892689124?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYWVkZWI2YS01NGRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_-uUZ_gLw-Mm0vrvZdw58eoQALT6sz7ykEdICoI035s=) I’ll keep showing up. I’ll keep fighting. And I’ll keep leading the way I always have — with strength, empathy, and a clear sense of right and wrong. **Thank you for being part of this—** Becca _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | **[$5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121552514/589824538/-892689124?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYWVkZWI2YS01NGRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_-uUZ_gLw-Mm0vrvZdw58eoQALT6sz7ykEdICoI035s=)** | **[$15](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121552515/589824539/-892689124?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYWVkZWI2YS01NGRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_-uUZ_gLw-Mm0vrvZdw58eoQALT6sz7ykEdICoI035s=)**

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I will not give up on our country
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
It has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
#

Folks,
If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. It didn’t stop when the FBI wanted to talk to me and other members of Congress. Because now the Department of Defense has announced that it is escalating its investigation into me.
All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear.
If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it.
I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None.
If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming.
And if you’d be willing to help support me as we gear up to fight back against the attacks coming our way from Donald Trump and his Administration, please:
[**Make a donation. It would mean a lot to me.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-dec25-dod/.FHtwNx&refcode=em251218-1-1m&t=1&akid=6303%2E6688874%2E8HalQD)

Friend, My sister-in-service Summer Lee needs our support. A true progressive, Summer is the first Black woman elected to Congress from the state of Pennsylvania. **She’s had to overcome millions of dollars in attacks from right-wing Trump donors, including anti-Palestinian and pro-genocide group AIPAC** , in order to win and retain her seat in Congress. **Summer just launched her re-election campaign and we need to ensure her continued leadership in Congress.** Together, we just launched the first-ever Environmental Justice Caucus in the House and we have so much work to do to fight fascism and corporate greed. [**Read more about one of my fiercest allies below. But if you already know that you support Summer Lee, click here to split a donation between our re-election campaigns today.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJA3BHgOo_X6hGJL4izAkUbf6Lbonzvt6UP7Uqy7QyZZbN7gv7rJMm7ptMag1TQ_a3KrdEjKp9PXJXdhNh5zU4-IP92DBVmx5Zjywq_B_jKjUedyL1__ouaH95JRlFptDhUWHVR8YsP6L3Y3Ds4OXBFkwfPZP9c9Ebylz1uFBYFlUGxSZyTIzQEm78EC7QY_E0xKq5FOrg6YVWcE9Wm0MwKwv5b1TpKLfc2tCBJjBr-hfvlHqK0IMYK44LeHRBmN_iaQc2UtZy2kRj5UHSe67hr-HqnwbvtthF9ArKnjfOrb0LZidRm2tAwdXs1MYNjHYy5ce7QgY5a4hv9dhGvwnInGIQUb53EPcMLBnd0vOXikjcSDRPmT4iAH24o1tePbtt98VTwWkgumckukBrjnUo5QroKUvUnY6seGgeWDvTfWQgRoT_ecvMo4J7676VGadTovU7x7oAC70kVD1jCHJG5g/4mj/aNuZvVXbSuK2m31tmEXvRQ/h1/h001.JsEsRCpElv8FuR5bsLAletJEcMldgUwPSDyqNv7UIkk) **Summer and I have co-led on many critical issues.** I’ve joined her in sponsoring her **_OLIGARCH Act_** to take on extreme billionaire wealth. And she’s joined me on my **_Justice for All Act_** to strengthen our civil rights laws and defend LGBTQ+ people.
Summer is a leading voice in abolishing super PACs, **demanding reparations for Black folks,** and **taking on our country’s racist and classist cash bail system** —ensuring everyone’s right to due process.
**This past summer, Summer was one of just three members of Congress to join me in voting for an amendment to block $600 million in U.S. military aid to the apartheid Israeli government.**
Summer is a consistent ally in our fight for human rights―at home and abroad. She’s also holding Trump accountable, leading the subpoena to release the Epstein files and championing policy to reinstate White House ethics rules.
But because of her bold leadership, she’ll once again be targeted by right-wing billionaires and PACs. That’s why she needs our early support, to prepare for the fight ahead.

clean_body: Summer just launched her re-election campaign and we need to ensure her continued leadership in Congress. | | | Friend, My sister-in-service Summer Lee needs our support. A true progressive, Summer is the first Black woman elected to Congress from the state of Pennsylvania. She’s had to overcome millions of dollars in attacks from right-wing Trump donors, including anti-Palestinian and pro-genocide group AIPAC , in order to win and retain her seat in Congress. Summer just launched her re-election campaign and we need to ensure her continued leadership in Congress. Together, we just launched the first-ever Environmental Justice Caucus in the House and we have so much work to do to fight fascism and corporate greed. | Summer and I have co-led on many critical issues. I’ve joined her in sponsoring her OLIGARCH Act to take on extreme billionaire wealth. And she’s joined me on my Justice for All Act to strengthen our civil rights laws and defend LGBTQ+ people. Summer is a leading voice in abolishing super PACs, demanding reparations for Black folks, and taking on our country’s racist and classist cash bail system —ensuring everyone’s right to due process. This past summer, Summer was one of just three members of Congress to join me in voting for an amendment to block $600 million in U.S. military aid to the apartheid Israeli government. Summer is a consistent ally in our fight for human rights―at home and abroad. She’s also holding Trump accountable, leading the subpoena to release the Epstein files and championing policy to reinstate White House ethics rules. But because of her bold leadership, she’ll once again be targeted by right-wing billionaires and PACs. That’s why she needs our early support, to prepare for the fight ahead. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split evenly between Summer Lee and Rashida Tlaib | | | | | | | | | Thank you for your support, Rashida Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . clean_body: You've seen 2025 "wrapped" from Spotify, Facebook, Google, Uber, Apple, and Amazon. What about what WE accomplished! | Spotify. Facebook. Google. Uber. Apple. Amazon. Fellow American, you've seen these companies share your highlights of 2025. Where you've been. Your top played songs. Where you've ordered food. But this is a little different. I would like to hear from you about what Senate Republicans and President Trump accomplished this year since taking back the Senate majority. Here's a reminder of some of our top achievements (). Conservative Wins in 2025: Under President Trump and Senate Republicans, we added nearly 700,000 new jobs for Americans, while seeing an overall stabilization of food prices and actual reductions in gas prices. I was proud to support the largest tax cut in history and deliver on our promise to support President Trump's America First agenda. It secures the border, strengthens our national security, unleashes American energy, reduces wasteful spending, and cuts taxes for working families AND seniors. We are better off because of it. We had record-low illegal border crossings in 2025. We kept our promises to keep dangerous drugs out of our country and protect our communities after years of open borders. We finally have a Senate and a president who will unleash American energy independence, instead of pushing a radical environmental agenda. American energy ensures that we remain dominant on the world stage. But it doesn't end there. Please make sure to respond before the year is over. Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Senator Steve Daines | | Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

Hi, it’s Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read. I believe voting from home gives every Oregonian a fair and secure chance to hold politicians accountable—and it saves money and time, too. But some Republicans want to block voting from home by imposing unnecessary barriers. subject: BREAKING: Escalation from Pete Hegseth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
The Department of Defense is upgrading its preliminary review of Mark Kelly to an official investigation.
Peter, Mark Kelly here.
They are escalating.
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98596?refcode=em20251218-9843&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9843%2E614464%2EwCv6S8)
We hadn't heard anything significant since December 10th, and then they dropped this.
Honestly, we learned about this the same as everyone else. We found out that Trump called for my arrest and execution by tweet. We heard about this investigation for the first time by tweet. And now that they're escalating, we hear it by tweet — again.
Trump and Hegseth won't let this go — but I refuse to back down. And I need you with me to have any chance against them.

name: Mark Kelly
subject: My decision...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121553273/589827253/1545041525?amount=10&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.18_Mark-Kelly-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNzFhZWNlZS01NWRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dRrGekWFV_TvRDnPSP5I_ojHj_ToUFyC2ekpFM7Fh_E=) Hey folks, it’s Mark Kelly. Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate. I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy. But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something. So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too. I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help. We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do. **[I’m asking you to split a donation between Kirsten Gillibrand and me, and I hope you know that I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t important.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121553274/589827254/1545041525?amount=10&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.18_Mark-Kelly-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNzFhZWNlZS01NWRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dRrGekWFV_TvRDnPSP5I_ojHj_ToUFyC2ekpFM7Fh_E=)** _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Mark Kelly and Kirsten Gillibrand will go through immediately:_ | | [DONATE $10 NOW >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121553275/589827255/1545041525?amount=10&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.18_Mark-Kelly-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNzFhZWNlZS01NWRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dRrGekWFV_TvRDnPSP5I_ojHj_ToUFyC2ekpFM7Fh_E=)
