name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: About AI data centers and utility costs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Big Tech passing the costs of their AI gamble onto consumers is plain wrong.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2730%2E4032634%2Em1yJ-r)
---
Big Tech companies are rapidly building AI data centers that require more and more energy from our grid. In fact, it’s estimated that a typical AI data center uses the same amount of electricity as 100,000 households **annually.** So utility companies across the country are spending billions of dollars updating their infrastructure to support the unprecedented energy demands coming from these new AI data centers. Now, you may assume that the Big Tech companies would be responsible for covering the costs necessary to accommodate all of the energy they’re gobbling up. Think again. The utility companies are recouping those costs by raising consumers’ (cough, cough: YOUR) household utility bills instead — forcing American families to bankroll the electricity costs of trillion-dollar tech companies. To add insult to injury, many data centers are receiving discounted rates on energy as the utility companies are trying to attract their business. **I’ve opened an investigation into the extent to which Big Tech AI data centers are driving up your electricity costs, and I’ve sent inquiries to Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, CoreWeave, Digital Reality, and Equinix demanding answers on their energy usage and costs being passed to consumers.** [**Add your name to co-sign my letter demanding transparency from Big Tech companies who expect everyday consumers to foot the bill of their AI gamble.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/ew-em-bigtechdatacenters/?source=EMZ28X_TEC_E_20251218_V1S1&t=8&akid=2730%2E4032634%2Em1yJ-r) | [**ADD YOUR NAME**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/ew-em-bigtechdatacenters/?source=EMZ28X_TEC_E_20251218_V1S1&t=9&akid=2730%2E4032634%2Em1yJ-r)
---
I’m deeply concerned about the impact of data centers on local communities, and the strain they put on resources like land, water, and electricity. And it’s a strain that will only get worse as these companies double down on AI investments. Case in point: The Department of Energy estimates that data centers could make up 12% of all U.S. power consumption by 2028.
Meanwhile, Donald “I’ll Lower Costs On Day One” Trump seems unconcerned about your ballooning energy bill. In fact, he signed an executive order to prevent states from regulating AI in a clear giveaway to his Big Tech megadonors.
Contracts between data centers and utility companies are almost always confidential, which leaves the public in the dark on why energy costs are skyrocketing, and how much Big Tech companies are paying for it. That’s why we need answers and transparency from Big Tech companies.
[**Add your name: Join me in demanding that Big Tech companies must pay their fair share for the electrical grid updates made specifically to accommodate their data centers’ energy needs.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/ew-em-bigtechdatacenters/?source=EMZ28X_TEC_E_20251218_V1S1&t=10&akid=2730%2E4032634%2Em1yJ-r)
Thanks for raising your voice on this important issue. I’m going to stay on it, and I’ll keep you posted.
— Elizabeth

---
| | | |
---

---

body:
Your voice belongs in this next chapter of our movement.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121557657/589841190/1328794144?refcode=ea.c83009dc-64dc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.c83009dc-64dc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM4MzAwOWRjLTY0ZGMtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=4CrHam0AmOJ8OuPtx7-BwJH_fnxb17fMOpWHhWvvVXQ=&emci=579b6c2c-57dc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c83009dc-64dc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=4823105)

As I look back on our historic victories this year, I keep thinking about what it took to elect more than 60 bold climate champions across the country. None of it would have happened without grassroots supporters like you. **Your support doesn't go unnoticed.** Now, we're turning our attention to 2026, and I'll be honest — it's going to be one hell of a fight for our climate movement. That’s why my team and I are making some big decisions right now. We’re taking a hard look at what’s working, what needs to shift, and how JanePAC can rise to meet this political moment with the strength and clarity it demands. **_[We've created a brief Post-Election Priorities Survey to gather input from supporters like you who've been with us on this journey. Will you take a moment to share your thoughts? name: New Dems
subject: MAGA Republicans hold all the levers of power but we can change that >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund
body:
Our hands are tied

| It’s been less than a year under the Trump administration and the consequences of their reckless policies are already clear:

_MAGA Republicans passed the largest cut to Medicaid in history.

For the first time in decades, Social Security is at risk for complete dissolution.

MAGA Republicans broke the record for the longest government shutdown.

Americans are struggling to pay their rent, utilities, and groceries._

**[We can’t afford to wait and hope things get better. Will you chip in $10 to help us hold them accountable for their failures by flipping the House and reinstating commonsense leadership? >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121558017/589842667/-174747403?amount=10&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,250,500&refcode=NDCAF_20251218_EMDF_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjM2U4NDE1Mi02NWRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EpWTlGJag_QCMKfb_MCUQShwSn3_dcRon9iH9yVNDqE=)** | [CHIP IN JUST $10 TO HELP US ELECT A NEW DEM MAJORITY IN 2026 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121558018/589842668/-174747403?amount=10&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,250,500&refcode=NDCAF_20251218_EMDF_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjM2U4NDE1Mi02NWRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EpWTlGJag_QCMKfb_MCUQShwSn3_dcRon9iH9yVNDqE=)

subject: It’s unbelievable.

body:
Watching the chaos caused by Republican leadership this year has been unbelievable.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121558079/589842844/-153530995?refcode=EM_F_251218unbelievable_N_SB_90DO_5_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYmU4NDE1Mi02NWRjLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_LuLJWXNl0cC0qWOsnDGjYIgGaR2o__AuovAR25fIe0=) It has been a long year, to say the least.

Peter – 2025 will come to an end two weeks from today, and watching the chaos caused by Republican leadership this year has been unbelievable.

Republicans have been attacking our communities and our democracy from every angle – from abusing the national guard and tearing families apart to so-called “making America healthy again” by undermining vaccines, allowing asbestos in makeup, promoting misinformation around reproductive rights, and making prices for hardworking Americans skyrocket.

It’s impressive, really, all the harm that they have been able to do while also being resentful that they even have to show up to work.

[**We can’t let this continue. In 2026, we need to be ready to hit the ground running to unseat Republicans and take back the House. Will you join her and be the change we need? ******=****** Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress Shontel Brown for Congress 545 E Town Street Columbus, OH 43215 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

subject: Why was this federal judge at a MAGA rally?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251218-61006&t=1001&akid=61006%2E1407376%2EIvNYH9 ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251218-61006&t=1002&akid=61006%2E1407376%2EIvNYH9 ]Demand Justice PAC

Trump-appointed federal judge Emil Bove was just seen at a MAGA campaign
rally — smirking as Donald Trump called to deport Rep. Ilhan Omar.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251218.X-mUTo&refcode=em20251218-61006&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61006%2E1407376%2EIvNYH9 ]Demand Justice: Emil Bove Attended Trump Rally, Breaking Judicial Code
of Conduct.

This isn’t a gray area. Bove just openly violated the judicial code of
conduct and ethics rules that ban judges from engaging in partisan
political activity.

Enough is enough. This is why Demand Justice is demanding all Senate
Democrats sign a pledge to vote NO on Trump cronies who are nominated to
the bench.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251218.X-mUTo&refcode=em20251218-61006&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1004&akid=61006%2E1407376%2EIvNYH9 ]Chip in now to help Demand Justice stop Trump from putting more loyal
cronies like Emil Bove on the federal bench.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251218.X-mUTo&amount=5&refcode=em20251218-61006&t=1023&akid=61006%2E1407376%2EIvNYH9 ]Donate $5 ››

Judges are supposed to be independent. They’re supposed to uphold the rule
of law, not show up at campaign-style rallies for the politician who put
them on the bench.

But Trump’s loyalist judges increasingly act like ethics rules don’t apply
to them.

Why would they? They’ve learned there are no consequences.

This is what court capture looks like.

Demand Justice exists to call this out. We expose judicial corruption
under Trump, and fight back against a system that’s being rigged in plain
sight. We pressure lawmakers. We mobilize the public. And we refuse to let
these abuses become normalized. But we can’t do it without you.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55545?c0=dj-em-20251218.-e40um&refcode=em20251218-61006&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1024&akid=61006%2E1407376%2EIvNYH9 ]Give just $5 to help Demand Justice protect the courts and rule of law
from Trump. We’re relying on grassroots donors like you.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251218.X-mUTo&amount=5&refcode=em20251218-61006&t=1043&akid=61006%2E1407376%2EIvNYH9 ]Donate $5 ››

In solidarity,

Demand Justice PAC

subject: This isn’t a game

body:
[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1001&akid=5157%2E35263%2ET7HTLz ]TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE

+------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| |
| |
|Higher cost, worse coverage: Affordable Care Act enrollees say expiring |
| subsidies will hit them hard |
| |
| — AP News, December 2025 |
| |
| Kelley Bruns said she is concerned that if something happens to their |
| health in the next year, they could go bankrupt. While their monthly |
| fees are low at about $25, their new out-of-pocket maximum at $21,000 |
| amounts to nearly half their joint income. |
| |
| “We have to pray that we don’t have to have surgery or don’t have to |
|have some medical procedure done that we’re not aware of,” she said. “It|
| would be very devastating.” |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------+

The plan that Mike Johnson and the Republican party have released does
nothing to protect the ACA subsidies that keep health care affordable for
millions of families nationwide.

This isn’t a partisan issue — folks on both sides of the aisle, whether
they voted red or blue last election, agree that protecting these
subsidies is crucial to keeping hospitals open and health care affordable.

We’re heading into the last legislative week of the year; Republicans are
running out of time. That’s why we need to fight for a Democratic majority
that will put the American people FIRST.

clean_body: [ ]TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE +------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | | | | |Higher cost, worse coverage: Affordable Care Act enrollees say expiring | | subsidies will hit them hard | | | | — AP News, December 2025 | | | | Kelley Bruns said she is concerned that if something happens to their | | health in the next year, they could go bankrupt. While their monthly | | fees are low at about $25, their new out-of-pocket maximum at $21,000 | | amounts to nearly half their joint income. | | | | “We have to pray that we don’t have to have surgery or don’t have to | |have some medical procedure done that we’re not aware of,” she said. “It| | would be very devastating.” | +------------------------------------------------------------------------+ The plan that Mike Johnson and the Republican party have released does nothing to protect the ACA subsidies that keep health care affordable for millions of families nationwide. This isn’t a partisan issue — folks on both sides of the aisle, whether they voted red or blue last election, agree that protecting these subsidies is crucial to keeping hospitals open and health care affordable. We’re heading into the last legislative week of the year; Republicans are running out of time. That’s why we need to fight for a Democratic majority that will put the American people FIRST. name: Matthew Davis, LCV
subject: DEVELOPING: Senate votes to sell out the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge’s ENTIRE Coastal Plain.
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTMzMTY0NzIS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjM2MDIyNTY1NAS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/mkyVHmA- We’re running out of time, Peter. Hundreds of people from around the country have stepped up today for Giving Tuesday, but we’re still running short of the 1,000 members we need to unlock an additional $100,000 for our work before midnight tonight. And with the Trump administration accelerating some of the most extreme anti-environment, pro-polluter attacks in modern history,falling short is simply not an option. We’re asking you, Peter: will you renew your LCV membership right now while membership is only $5 to help us unlock an additional $100,000 to oppose the Trump administration’s attempts to exploit our public lands and roll back climate progress? https://links.messages.lcv. This isn’t just about hitting a number. It’s about having the resources we need to challenge these increasingly reckless orders and attempts to shred environmental safeguards — and stop the polluter handouts that Trump and his allies are fast-tracking. RENEW YOUR MEMBERSHIP >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/WabwQyjrD8c_ Every single person who steps up today is critical to our efforts — here’s why this Giving Tuesday is so important: Trump is trying to erase the scientific and legal foundation of climate action. By dismantling the Endangerment Finding, which affirms that greenhouse gas emissions harm our health and welfare, Trump’s EPA would hand fossil fuel companies a free pass to spew climate pollution into our air. He’s also ending the program that required big polluters to report their pollution, making it nearly impossible to track what they’re spewing or hold them accountable.
Decades of established pollution protections are being peeled away. More than a dozen rules that keep toxic pollution out of the air we breathe and water we drink to protect our health are on the chopping block, tossed aside to allow fossil fueled power plants to cut corners and shift the burden of their damage onto our communities.
The Trump administration is giving away the Arctic to corporate polluters, working to allow more drilling in one of the planet’s last intact ecosystems. Mining roads carved through national parks and preserves. Fire-sale auctions in critical ecosystems. Hundreds of millions of acres of U.S. waters off Alaska potentially opened to drilling. It could do irreparable damage to wildlife, our climate, and Alaska Native communities. We’re working every day with our 30 state affiliates, organizing on the ground, exposing backroom deals and corruption, rallying voters, and defending the treasured and unique places that define our country. But meeting this moment requires resources — especially as we go up against big polluters and fossil fuel opponents. This $100,000 match challenge, and how many people step up this Giving Tuesday, will determine how hard we can push back. Peter, your renewal right now powers our work to protect people and the planet. Will you renew before MIDNIGHT TONIGHT to help keep this movement strong when it matters most? $5 is all it takes. https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/EZ_ Every contribution brings us closer. And the clock is ticking. Thanks for standing with us, Chris Brooks
Vice President of Membership
League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=13316472&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjM2MDIyNTY1NAS2&spReportId=MjM2MDIyNTY1NAS2.Click here to unsubscribe https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=13316472&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjM2MDIyNTY1NAS2&spReportId=MjM2MDIyNTY1NAS2 from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

740 15th St NW, Suite 700
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683 DONATE https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/L6b_

name: Nick Melvoin
subject: "How the Phone Ban Saved High School"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nick Melvoin for School Board 2026, 1787 Tribute Road, Suite K, Sacramento, CA, 95815
body:
| | | | | [|
---](https://nickmelvoin.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e47425f168c0b7b00df186831&id=d4bbf15954&e=3cf484ad56)
| | | | | |

Friends,
Have you seen the articles coming out every few days like this one, ["How the Phone Ban Saved High School”](https://nickmelvoin.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e47425f168c0b7b00df186831&id=d2cc327d66&e=3cf484ad56)?
When I brought the resolution to ban phones in LAUSD almost two years ago, I hoped it would have a real impact on students’ focus, mental health, and school culture—not just in LA, but around the country. Seeing stories like this now has been affirming.
But it’s also made me think about what comes next.
Phones were the first step. Now we need to think more seriously about technology in schools overall and how to strike the right balance between screens, play, creativity, and human connection. I was recently featured in an [NBC News piece](https://nickmelvoin.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e47425f168c0b7b00df186831&id=f963cdd2c0&e=3cf484ad56) digging into exactly that question around iPads and Chromebooks in LA schools:
[](https://nickmelvoin.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e47425f168c0b7b00df186831&id=841afd3cd8&e=3cf484ad56)One of the reasons I’m running for a final term on the school board is because there’s more work to do to support students’ mental health in this new digital age.
If you agree, I’d really appreciate your help. Can you chip in before December 31st to support this campaign?
Every contribution — [$25, $50, $100, or whatever you’re able](https://nickmelvoin.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e47425f168c0b7b00df186831&id=56233dbe00&e=3cf484ad56) — helps make sure we can keep pushing this work forward.
Thanks, as always, for your support and partnership.

---
| | | | | | | | | | | [DONATE TODAY](https://nickmelvoin.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e47425f168c0b7b00df186831&id=70ce53b909&e=3cf484ad56)
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | Onward,

clean_body: Come November, District One could well decide who controls the House Majority. | | | | Team, come November, District One could well decide who controls the House Majority. National Republicans know that they’re slipping. In a desperate attempt to hold onto power, they’re spending millions to try to flip this seat, but you know me. I’m a fighter for Southern Nevada and I won’t back down. Republican Speaker Johnson thinks that by cozying up to far-right mega donors he can keep his extreme majority in power. Our democracy should not work that way. Super PACs, mega donors, and special interest groups should not decide who leads our country — the voters should. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | Thank you, Dina The Nevada State Democratic Party fights for progress in the Silver State. We believe that we are stronger together than on our own, and that America succeeds when everyone gets a fair shot, everyone does their fair share, and everyone plays by the same rules. | | Paid for by the Nevada State Democratic Party, NVDEMS.com, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. With this new development, we need to urgently update our records: **NAME:[dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71081?t=4&akid=23746%2E4449941%2EjN7CPD)

name: Sarah McBride
subject: What I said on the Capitol steps
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
I need you standing with me.
#
I want to share what I shared on the Capitol steps yesterday:
I didn’t have the courage to come out until I was 21.
The truth is, I had known who I was for my entire life, and that meant 21 years of pain. 21 years of a kind of homesickness that only went away when I was finally able to get the care I needed.
My biggest regret in life is that I never had a childhood without that pain.
So many years later, I marvel at the courage of transgender young people today, who are sharing themselves with their families and this world despite the toxicity and the hate that too often comes from Congress and from the politicians inside it.
All any of us want is to live a life of purpose and happiness and wholeness, and I carry that with me every day in Congress — especially right now.
**Because Mike Johnson and House Republicans are pushing two anti-trans votes this week. One bill risks jailing parents and doctors for affirming transgender children and following medical best practices. That bill passed the House last night.**
Republican politicians have made their priorities painfully clear: all they care about is making the rich richer and attacking trans people.
As the only openly trans member of Congress, I refuse to stay silent. I will keep fighting for the dignity, humanity, and fair treatment of every person in Delaware and across the country.
**But if we’re going to tackle this Republican obsession, I need you standing with me.**
[**Rush a donation to help me keep pushing back. Every donation sends a powerful message that we won’t let Republicans get away with weaponizing trans kids as political pawns.**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251218-1.IsnwIH&refcode=em20251218-2599&t=1&akid=2599%2E21716%2E3A0cGQ)
**[Donate »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251218-1.IsnwIH&refcode=em20251218-2599&t=20&akid=2599%2E21716%2E3A0cGQ)**
Thank you,
—Sarah
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20251218-1&t=21&refcode2=2599_21716_3A0cGQ&akid=2599%2E21716%2E3A0cGQ)**

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 12/18/2025
body:
Colorado’s Budget Is Bigger Than Ever. Health Care Is Why.
| | | | [](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
| | Hi friends,
Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.joinrmv.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.joinrmv.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.joinrmv.). Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** A data breach knocked out Douglas County’s [CodeRED emergency alerts](https://link.email.joinrmv.) as [fire danger rose](https://link.email.joinrmv.), forcing officials to scramble for a new system. Denver Public Schools’ [continued enrollment decline](https://link.email.joinrmv.) exposed a deeper budget crisis beyond school closures, while new analysis showed [Colorado’s budget is larger than ever](https://link.email.joinrmv.)—driven largely by health care spending now consuming nearly half of state appropriations.
🇺🇸**On the National Stage...** The Trump administration moved to break up Boulder-based NCAR, accusing the federally funded lab of [driving climate alarmism](https://link.email.joinrmv.) and signaling a shift away from Green New Deal–style research. Meanwhile, an investigative report alleged a [major Democrat donation-laundering scheme](https://link.email.joinrmv.) involving networks tied to Gov. Gretchen Whitmer and Sens. Jon Ossoff and Cory Booker, raising renewed concerns about campaign finance enforcement.
🗣️**Commentary Spotlight...** Commentary this week warns that unelected regulators are steering Colorado policy without voter consent—highlighting a little-known transportation climate rule now [shaping highway projects](https://link.email.joinrmv.), a state commission blocking [expanded emissions reviews for businesses](https://link.email.joinrmv.), and a growing double standard where [delay and endless process](https://link.email.joinrmv.) are used to advance policies voters never approved.
This is the Mountain Minute for Thursday, December 18, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Colorado’s Budget Is Bigger Than Ever. Health Care Is Why.](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
[](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
Colorado’s state budget is larger than it used to be. That much isn’t disputed.
What has changed over the last twenty years is where that growth landed.
The [Common Sense Institute’s “Colorado Budget: Then and Now” (December 2025)](https://link.email.joinrmv.) Colorado’s state budget has grown faster than population and inflation since the mid-2000s. The shift wasn’t sudden. It accumulated, year by year, across multiple budgets and multiple administrations.
The increase shows up clearly in the numbers. In the mid-2000s, state spending worked out to a little under $5,600 per person once population and inflation were accounted for. It didn’t stay there. Year by year, the number crept higher. It now sits above $7,300. The increase didn’t happen overnight. It crept in, budget by budget.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**[Delay as policy: A double standard Colorado must answer](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY RMV EDITORIAL BOARD, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
* **Colorado argues delay is illegal—except when it uses it:** The state is suing the federal government for slow-walking EV funding, claiming agencies cannot use delay to cancel programs lawmakers approved, even as Colorado regulators routinely use delay, review, and inaction to achieve policy outcomes voters never authorized.
* **Climate goals are steering decisions before rules even exist:** Records show state commissions treated Gov. Polis’ climate directives as binding guidance—allowing agencies to deny or stall lawful projects based on “intent” and emissions targets not yet written into statute or regulation.
* **Administrative delay has become a tool to shut down energy without a vote:** Instead of banning oil and gas outright, Colorado slowed permits, multiplied reviews, and shifted expectations mid-process—producing the same effect as a ban while sidestepping legislative accountability and public consent.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
|
---
**[The highway rule few Coloradans know is steering road projects](https://link.email.joinrmv.)** BY SCOTT JAMES, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
* **A little-known climate rule now controls Colorado’s highway decisions:** A GHG transportation rule written quietly during COVID forces every major road project to pass emissions modeling first—making climate math, not safety or congestion relief, the top priority in transportation planning.
* **Highway expansion is effectively penalized while transit is rewarded:** The rule nudges planners away from adding road capacity and toward transit, bike, and “mode-shift” projects, even when highways like I-25 are dangerously congested—driving up costs and delaying projects drivers actually need.
* **Public input was performative, not decisive:** Local officials and stakeholders warned the rule would hurt freight, rural commuters, and growing regions, but the outcome was pre-decided—turning “stakeholder meetings” into box-checking exercises while unelected regulators locked in the governor’s climate agenda.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
[****](https://link.email.joinrmv.)[**What Xcel promised regulators and what customers were told before Dec. 17 shutoff warnings**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
[****](https://link.email.joinrmv.)**BY JEN SCHUMANN | STATE | DECEMBER 16** _Coloradans are being asked to prepare for the chance of a planned outage on Dec. 17. The company’s public alerts tell people to watch the map. The filings tell a fuller story about thresholds, timelines and who is supposed to be in the loop when a shutoff is on the table._
______
[**When the State Disarms the Innocent, Violence Gets Time to Work**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
**BY C. J. GARBO | COMMENTARY | DECEMBER 16** _What happened in Australia was not merely a criminal act. It was a demonstration. A hard, visual lesson about time, power, and responsibility. Dozens of videos show attackers firing openly while innocent people run, hide, and plead._
[**The Bill of Rights was written to limit power. One civics lesson explains how.**](https://link.email.joinrmv.)
**BY JEN SCHUMANN | STATE | DECEMBER 15** _Bill of Rights Day is often marked with references to free speech, due process and other familiar rights. Less attention is paid to the reason those protections exist at all: to place clear limits on government power._[****](https://link.email.joinrmv.)__

**Feel like you have a unique story, commentary or tip to share?** Email [submit@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com).
|
---
|
---
🎅🏻 **DID YOU KNOW?** Coca-Cola played a major role in popularizing the **cheerful, red-suited image of Santa Claus** we recognize today.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "There is danger from all men. The only maxim of a free government ought to be to trust no man living with power to endanger the public liberty." – John Adams
|

# [When the Gibberish Becomes Policy](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182008911&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMDA4OTExLCJpYXQiOjE3NjYxMDM2NzUsImV4cCI6MTc2ODY5NTY3NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.BgGcOImQLVLE3ClDHhg8MbfJIWRXtRkBsuejcjVZNKM)
### Trump's latest speech, the Epstein files, and a media ecosystem that refuses to say the obvious
| | [Cliff Schecter](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge), [David Shuster](https://substack.com/@davidshuster), [Greg Olear](https://substack.com/@gregolear), and [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---
| Dec 19
---
| [](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge)| | [](https://substack.com/@davidshuster)| | [](https://substack.com/@gregolear)| | [](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---|---|---|---|---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182008911&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMDA4OTExLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NjEwMzY3NSwiZXhwIjoxNzY4Njk1Njc1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzcyMjUzMiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Wwet7Wa_IqoyXDsnoSWRmiUgdqdj2MuIfqYn39YH6BM&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182008911&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMDA4OTExLCJpYXQiOjE3NjYxMDM2NzUsImV4cCI6MTc2ODY5NTY3NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.BgGcOImQLVLE3ClDHhg8MbfJIWRXtRkBsuejcjVZNKM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=182008911&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMDA4OTExLCJpYXQiOjE3NjYxMDM2NzUsImV4cCI6MTc2ODY5NTY3NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.BgGcOImQLVLE3ClDHhg8MbfJIWRXtRkBsuejcjVZNKM)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/blueamp/p/the-trump-kennedy-center-wtf-cliff?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2e7d2f66-05a8-4d61-a046-e00c51498e8c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Some shows are about breaking news. Others are about stepping back and asking how the hell we got here. This one was firmly in that second category.
We started, as we so often do lately, with Donald Trump saying or doing something that would have been disqualifying in any other era. In this case, floating the absurd idea of renaming _The Kennedy Center_ after himself. As someone who's spent much of his life reading about and admiring the Kennedy legacy, this hit a f*king nerve.
Not just because it was offensive--oh it is--but because it captured something bigger: the way this dilapidated, purple-blotched nitwit treats history, culture, and institutions all as props for his own deeply-damaged ego.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8c5a6bfa-df6f-43fa-8924-094280465a72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
We pivoted into Trump's latest "speech"--more a chaotic, breathe-hole barfing than a coherent address, It was a stream of disconnected talking points, grievances, and numerical nonsense. Applying the word "thought" to that drivel would itself be a thought crime.
Drug prices reduced by "600 percent." Trillions in investment that don't exist. Shouting as if the mere decibel-level of his voice lent it authority. It's not just dishonesty anymore; **it 's deterioration**. What's most alarming isn't that Trump says these things--it's that so few people around him are either willing or able to stop him from embarrassing the office, the country, pretty much our entire way of life.
Greg Olear thankfully joined us, as I'm always grateful to have his perspective. Greg's one of those rare writers who writes like a poet but analyzes like management consultant. He helped frame the really bad reboot of _It_ we all watched as something deeper than a daily scandal.
We talked about how narcissism, impulsivity, and sociopathy don't mellow with age--they intensify. Add power, media enablers, a cult audience, and his elephantiasis of dementia, and you get the House of Cards isnanity we're seeing play out in real time.
* * *
Blue Amp Media is a reader-supported publication--subscribe for free!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.jCIVfpFn1a4vxy_vgxlYpYbSbeT_3zlEHfv7_0MZTj0?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=182008911)
* * *
A big part of the discussion centered on the Epstein files and the slow, drip-by-drip release of material tied to some powerful people. We weren't speculating--we were asking basic questions. Why release some photos? Why redact others? Who benefits from delay, confusion, or selective disclosure?
And perhaps most importantly: if the full truth doesn't come out officially, how likely is it that whistleblowers eventually force it into the open?
That led us straight into a media reckoning. We talked about the difference between reporters who clearly see the problem and feel compelled to normalize it. When the job becomes translating incoherent jabroni-blabber into "serious political analysis," democracy's in serious sh*t.
This isn't just about Fox News--it's about an oligarch-owned, monopoly-media ecosystem that sanitizes extremism and extreme-dumb because conflict drives clicks and accountability threatens access.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a819bb02-60ca-4779-81b2-2ca861518f5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
We've seen it in the way Fox and other right-wing sources put the spin on the recent [MAGA rebellion in the House on healthcare](https://substack.com/redirect/773616da-8180-40fe-ab7a-3da0a775eca0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), something I got to talk about on _**The Daily Whatever Show**_ on Friday as we [joked about how bad the news had been last week](https://substack.com/redirect/915d4b9e-e4fe-45bc-913b-03c66828466a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
We also spent time on something that doesn't get enough attention: culture. _Greg made a sharp point about the decline of the humanities and how imagination, critical thinking, and storytelling matter more than ever_. You can't recognize authoritarian nonsense if you've never trained your brain to question narrative propaganda.
Which brought us, fittingly, to Rob Reiner and the enduring relevance of films like _A Few Good Men_ and _The American President_. Those monologues weren't just great writing--they were warnings. [So I'm proud of this article I wrote about just that topic](https://substack.com/redirect/1de345c1-ba3c-4001-8782-02bf81730855?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[Culture](https://substack.com/redirect/982d6876-22f5-43e0-a84d-8b87ceb249ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
## [How Rob Reiner Decoded Our Politics In 2 Great Movie Monologues](https://substack.com/redirect/982d6876-22f5-43e0-a84d-8b87ceb249ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| | [Cliff Schecter](https://substack.com/profile/31941177-cliff-schecter) and [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/profile/312330913-blue-amp)| | *
---|---|---
| Dec 18
[](https://substack.com/redirect/982d6876-22f5-43e0-a84d-8b87ceb249ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/982d6876-22f5-43e0-a84d-8b87ceb249ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/982d6876-22f5-43e0-a84d-8b87ceb249ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
We closed by looking into the future. Not one where we don't need roads--sadly that one seems further off than predicted. But the 2026 elections. Democratic leadership that can feel tired and reactive. And the growing hunger--especially among younger voters--for politicians who _sound like they believe what they 're saying_.
Whether that energy translates into real change remains an open question. But it certainly can if we make enough noise, make our demands clear, and we do some heavy lifting on the messaging--it is the digital age, we can reach as far as our ideas will take us.
Pretending this moment is normal, though, is the one thing we absolutely cannot afford to do. So let's not do it. Let's change it.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2e7d2f66-05a8-4d61-a046-e00c51498e8c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

That’s exactly the kind of “leadership” that hurts Wisconsinites, putting special interests and billionaires first before working Wisconsinites. **[Can I count on you to chip in $15 or $27 to our grassroots campaign?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_121825_B1_Loophole&t=3&akid=5724%2E196590%2EWpuPzE)** _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now._ | | | [DONATE $15 »](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/6574?amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_121825_B1_Loophole&t=4&akid=5724%2E196590%2EWpuPzE)

This “billionaire loophole” was passed over a decade ago. In 2015, the state legislature made monumental changes to our state’s campaign finance system, allowing unlimited money to influence our politics.
And unfortunately, we’ve seen the consequences of loopholes like these: billionaires are buying power and influence, while working families have to bear the burden.
The contrast in this race couldn’t be clearer. When push came to shove, Tiffany catered to special interests and outside donors instead of what was right for Wisconsin.
I support a political system that allows every Wisconsinite’s voice to be heard, not to be drowned out by out-of-state billionaires.
**[I’m asking you to join my campaign, where we are laser-focused on delivering for Wisconsin: lowering costs, expanding Badgercare, and ensuring everyone is represented fairly in our government.](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_121825_B1_Loophole&t=10&akid=5724%2E196590%2EWpuPzE)**

Mandela Barnes

subject: Why Eye Drops Fail (But This Works)
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e26445794/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvNUpLS2pCREJBRg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMTg/LbmNsS0trU29SeA/qP2hpZHM9bmNtcEJTYVpsaXdhenJscw/gaUSTxQ/s5r40267792)

---
| | | **Dry Eyes? The Problem Isn’t on the Surface**
If you’re constantly reaching for eye drops, you’re not alone—but you might be treating the _wrong problem_.
Most Americans are told to “just use drops,” yet chronic dry eye often begins inside the body—rooted in inflammation and a lack of critical omega-3s. That’s why drops offer a few minutes of comfort... and then the burning, itching, and irritation return.
What actually makes a difference? Not generic fish oil—your body can’t absorb enough of it. The key is **high-absorption omega-3s paired with the antioxidant astaxanthin** , shown to support natural tear production, calm irritation, and protect delicate eye tissue.
It’s no wonder thousands of Americans are finally finding real, lasting relief—without living bottle-to-bottle on eye drops.
If you’re tired of temporary fixes, this short report explains the truth behind dry eye—and the solution that’s helping so many people:
[👉](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e26445794/VaHR0cHM6Ly9mYi5uYXRpdmVwYXRoLmNvbS9yZC9yLnBocA/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMTg/LbmNsS0trU29SeA/qP3NpZD0xNzE0JnB1Yj0yNDA1NzMmYzE9MjAyNTEyMTctQUZXUi0wJmMyPUFJREUmYzM9QUZXUg/gaUSTxQ/s9p3f98847d) **[5 Reasons Drops Won’t Work (and What Actually Does)](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e26445794/VaHR0cHM6Ly9mYi5uYXRpdmVwYXRoLmNvbS9yZC9yLnBocA/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEyMTg/LbmNsS0trU29SeA/qP3NpZD0xNzE0JnB1Yj0yNDA1NzMmYzE9MjAyNTEyMTctQUZXUi0wJmMyPUFJREUmYzM9QUZXUg/gaUSTxQ/s9p3f98847d)**
